examTitle: AWS Certified DevOps Engineer - Professional (DOP-C02) Mock Exam

questions.question:
A company runs a production web application on Amazon ECS with the EC2 launch type. The DevOps team needs to deploy new versions with zero downtime and the ability to quickly roll back if a critical bug is detected.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Use CodeDeploy with the in-place deployment configuration and CodeDeployDefault.AllAtOnce
Use CodeDeploy with the blue/green deployment configuration and CodeDeployDefault.ECSLinear10PercentEvery1Minutes
Use Elastic Beanstalk with the All-at-Once deployment policy
Manually stop all ECS tasks and start new tasks with the updated image

questions.explanation: Blue/Green deployment với CodeDeploy cho ECS tạo task set mới song song, dịch chuyển traffic dần qua ALB listener và cho phép rollback nhanh bằng cách trỏ lại task set cũ. CodeDeployDefault.ECSLinear10PercentEvery1Minutes shift 10% traffic mỗi phút, an toàn cho production. In-place AllAtOnce gây downtime; Beanstalk All-at-Once cũng gây downtime; manual stop/start không có rollback nhanh.

questions.question:
A team uses AWS CodePipeline to deploy a serverless application across us-east-1 and eu-west-1. The pipeline source is in us-east-1, but the deployment stage must deploy to both regions.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Create two separate pipelines, one in each region, and trigger them via EventBridge
Use a single pipeline in us-east-1 with cross-region action and create an artifact bucket in eu-west-1
Use CodeBuild to copy artifacts manually to eu-west-1 before deployment
Create a pipeline in us-east-1 and use AWS DataSync to replicate artifacts to eu-west-1

questions.explanation: CodePipeline hỗ trợ cross-region actions native. Chỉ cần khai báo action với region khác và cung cấp artifact bucket ở region đó. CodePipeline tự copy artifact sang. Hai pipeline tách biệt khó đồng bộ; CodeBuild thủ công là anti-pattern; DataSync không phù hợp.

questions.question:
A DevOps engineer is configuring a CodeBuild project that needs to access an RDS database in a private subnet.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Configure the CodeBuild project to run in the same VPC, subnets, and security groups as the RDS instance
Attach an Internet Gateway to the CodeBuild project so it can reach the RDS endpoint
Use a NAT Gateway in the public subnet to allow CodeBuild to connect to the database
Move the RDS database to a public subnet temporarily during the build

questions.explanation: CodeBuild có VPC support: gán project vào VPC + private subnet + security group cho phép connect tới RDS. CodeBuild ENI sẽ được tạo trong subnet đó. Internet Gateway không phải để truy cập tài nguyên private; NAT chỉ dùng cho outbound internet; chuyển RDS public là sai bảo mật.

questions.question:
A company uses AWS CodeDeploy for Lambda function deployments. They want to deploy a new version with traffic shifting: 10% for 5 minutes, then 100%. If CloudWatch alarms trigger, deployment must automatically roll back.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which combination of steps should a DevOps engineer take to meet these requirements? (Choose TWO.)

options.text: Use deployment configuration CodeDeployDefault.LambdaCanary10Percent5Minutes
Use deployment configuration CodeDeployDefault.LambdaLinear10PercentEvery1Minute
Configure CloudWatch alarms in the CodeDeploy deployment group with automatic rollback enabled
Use S3 event notifications to monitor deployment progress
Create an EventBridge schedule to rerun the deployment after the traffic shift finishes.

questions.explanation: Canary deployment shift 10% rồi 100% sau N phút, đúng yêu cầu. Linear shift đều theo % mỗi khoảng (khác với canary). Trong deployment group bật alarm-based rollback để tự động roll back khi alarm fire. S3 events không liên quan.

questions.question:
An application is deployed using Elastic Beanstalk. The team needs to deploy frequent updates with absolute zero downtime, even during the deployment process, and they have budget for additional capacity.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: All at Once
Rolling
Rolling with Additional Batch
Immutable

questions.explanation: Immutable deploy tạo Auto Scaling Group mới song song với version cũ, chỉ swap khi mới healthy → zero downtime + rollback đơn giản (xóa ASG mới). Rolling reduces capacity tạm thời; Rolling with Additional Batch giảm tác động nhưng vẫn có overlap. All at Once gây downtime.

questions.question:
A DevOps team uses CodeCommit for source control. Developers in another AWS account need read-only access to a specific repository.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Create IAM users in the CodeCommit account and share credentials with the other account's developers
Create an IAM role in the CodeCommit account with codecommit:Get* and codecommit:List* permissions and allow the other account to assume it
Make the repository public and provide the HTTPS clone URL
Use CodeCommit triggers to copy the repository to S3 and share the bucket

questions.explanation: Cross-account access nên dùng IAM role với trust policy cho phép account khác AssumeRole, kèm permission read-only. Sharing credentials vi phạm best practice. CodeCommit không có khái niệm public repo. Copy ra S3 phá vỡ workflow Git.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. In a CodeDeploy deployment for EC2, the team wants to run database migration scripts BEFORE the new application version starts receiving traffic.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: ApplicationStop
BeforeInstall
BeforeAllowTraffic
ValidateService

questions.explanation: BeforeAllowTraffic chạy sau khi cài đặt nhưng trước khi load balancer cho phép traffic vào instance mới — đúng để chạy migration cuối cùng. ApplicationStop trước khi stop app cũ; BeforeInstall trước khi copy file; ValidateService sau khi traffic đã được allow (để verify).

questions.question:
A team builds a CI pipeline that needs to run unit tests, build a Docker image, and push it to Amazon ECR. The buildspec.yaml needs to have phases in the correct order.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: install → pre_build → build → post_build
pre_build → install → build → post_build
install → build → pre_build → post_build
build → install → pre_build → post_build

questions.explanation: Thứ tự chuẩn của CodeBuild buildspec: install (cài runtime, deps) → pre_build (login ECR, run tests) → build (docker build) → post_build (docker push, write image definition). Sai thứ tự sẽ fail.

questions.question:
An organization wants to enforce that all CodeCommit pull requests are approved by at least 2 reviewers before they can be merged.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Create an IAM policy that denies the GitPush action unless 2 tags are set
Create an approval rule template and associate it with the repository, requiring 2 approvers
Use CodePipeline manual approval action before the merge stage
Use a Lambda function triggered by CodeCommit events to validate approvers

questions.explanation: CodeCommit có Approval Rule Templates: định nghĩa số approver tối thiểu, gắn vào repo, áp dụng cho mọi PR. Đây là native feature, không cần Lambda hay IAM hack. CodePipeline manual approval áp dụng cho pipeline stage, không phải PR.

questions.question:
A team deploys Lambda functions using SAM. Each deployment must run integration tests against the new version, and if tests fail, traffic must NOT shift to the new version.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Use CodeDeploy with PreTraffic and PostTraffic Lambda hooks; place integration tests in PreTraffic hook
Use AWS Step Functions to orchestrate the deployment and test sequence
Add tests in the CodeBuild post_build phase before the CodeDeploy stage
Configure CloudWatch alarms only — they will automatically roll back if errors increase

questions.explanation: CodeDeploy cho Lambda hỗ trợ hooks: BeforeAllowTraffic (PreTraffic) chạy validation function trước khi shift traffic; nếu fail thì deployment fail và không shift. PostTraffic chạy sau khi shift xong. Tests trong build phase chạy trước deploy, không validate version mới đã deploy.

questions.question:
A company uses AWS CodeArtifact to manage internal Python packages. They want to ensure developers only consume approved packages from PyPI, and any new external package must be reviewed before being usable.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Disable the PyPI upstream repository entirely
Use CodeArtifact package origin controls to block direct uploads and prevent automatic ingestion from upstream
Set the repository to private and require IAM permissions for read access
Use S3 bucket policies on the underlying CodeArtifact bucket

questions.explanation: CodeArtifact package origin controls cho phép chặn ingestion từ upstream (PyPI) trừ khi admin allow từng package — đúng pattern cho review trước khi consume. Disable PyPI hoàn toàn quá strict; private repo không kiểm soát upstream; S3 bucket policy không expose ra.

questions.question:
A team uses CodePipeline with multiple environments (dev, staging, prod). They want prod deployment to require manual approval and the approver must verify a CloudWatch dashboard before approving.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Add a Manual Approval action in the prod stage and include the CloudWatch dashboard URL in the action's URL field
Use a Lambda invoke action that emails the dashboard image and waits for SES reply
Stop the pipeline at staging and create a new pipeline for prod manually
Use S3 source action to require uploading a signed approval file before continuing

questions.explanation: Manual Approval action có field URL for review để link tới CloudWatch dashboard / Grafana / runbook. Approver vào console, xem link, rồi approve. Pattern chuẩn và native. Lambda email approval phức tạp; tạo pipeline mới là sai pattern; S3 file approval không phải feature CodePipeline.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A Lambda function is deployed with versioning enabled. The team uses alias 'prod' pointing to version 5 (95%) and version 6 (5%) for canary testing. Errors spike on version 6.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Delete version 6 entirely from the Lambda console
Update the prod alias to route 100% traffic to version 5
Disable Lambda concurrency on version 6
Rebuild and redeploy version 5 as version 7

questions.explanation: Lambda alias hỗ trợ weighted routing. Để rollback ngay, update routing config: version 5 = 100%, version 6 = 0%. Một API call duy nhất. Xóa version có thể fail nếu đang chạy; concurrency = 0 cũng được nhưng làm error 429; redeploy mất thời gian build.

questions.question:
A DevOps engineer needs to run a CodeBuild job triggered on every commit to the main branch of a GitHub repository (not CodeCommit).

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Use a Lambda function polling GitHub API every 5 minutes and triggering CodeBuild
Configure a CodeBuild webhook with FILE_PATH and BRANCH filters in the project source settings
Use AWS CodeStar to mirror GitHub to CodeCommit and trigger on CodeCommit events
Use CodePipeline with a GitHub v1 source action and CloudWatch scheduled rule

questions.explanation: CodeBuild có native GitHub webhook support với filter groups (event = PUSH, branch = main). GitHub gọi webhook khi có commit, CodeBuild chạy ngay — không cần polling. Lambda poll API là anti-pattern; mirror sang CodeCommit phức tạp; scheduled rule không phải event-driven.

questions.question:
A company uses CodePipeline to deploy a CloudFormation stack. After the CREATE_CHANGE_SET action, the pipeline should pause for approval, then execute the change set only if approved.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: CreateChangeSet → ManualApproval → ExecuteChangeSet
ManualApproval → CreateChangeSet → ExecuteChangeSet
ExecuteChangeSet → ManualApproval → CreateChangeSet
CreateChangeSet → ExecuteChangeSet → ManualApproval

questions.explanation: Đúng pattern: tạo change set (xem trước thay đổi), pause cho người duyệt, sau đó execute nếu OK. CloudFormation action types trong CodePipeline có: CHANGE_SET_REPLACE/CREATE, CHANGE_SET_EXECUTE, CREATE_UPDATE, DELETE_ONLY, REPLACE_ON_FAILURE.

questions.question:
A team builds container images using CodeBuild and pushes to ECR. They need image vulnerability scanning to block the pipeline if critical vulnerabilities are found.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Enable ECR Enhanced Scanning, use EventBridge to trigger a Lambda that fails the CodePipeline action when CRITICAL findings exist
Run Trivy in the CodeBuild build phase and exit with non-zero code on critical findings
Use Amazon Inspector to scan the EC2 build host
Use GuardDuty to scan the image after push

questions.explanation: ECR Enhanced Scanning (powered by Inspector) scan continuous + emit findings tới EventBridge. Lambda đọc finding, gọi PutJobFailureResult để fail action trong pipeline. Trivy in-build cũng work nhưng kém integrated; Inspector EC2 host không scan image; GuardDuty không scan container image (GuardDuty for ECR runtime is malware behavior detection).

questions.question:
A DevOps engineer wants to share artifacts between two CodeBuild projects in a pipeline. Project A produces a JAR file that Project B needs to test.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Project A uploads the JAR to a personal S3 bucket; Project B downloads via aws s3 cp
Use the artifacts section in buildspec.yaml to output files, and pass between actions as CodePipeline input/output artifacts
Mount a shared EFS volume on both CodeBuild projects
Email the JAR file between build phases

questions.explanation: CodePipeline quản lý artifacts qua artifact store (S3 bucket). Project A output artifact → Project B input artifact. Pipeline tự download/upload, có versioning, encryption. Tự quản lý S3 phức tạp và sai pattern; EFS overkill; email là joke.

questions.question:
A company manages 50 AWS accounts in an AWS Organization. They want to deploy a baseline CloudFormation stack (IAM roles, S3 logging bucket) to all accounts in 3 regions.

All infrastructure changes must be reproducible from approved templates or managed configuration documents. The platform team wants to reduce manual console changes and keep a clear audit trail.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Use CloudFormation StackSets with service-managed permissions and deploy to the organization root
Create 50 separate CloudFormation stacks manually in each account
Use AWS Config conformance packs with a custom rule for each resource
Write a Python script using boto3 to iterate accounts and deploy stacks

questions.explanation: StackSets với service-managed permissions tích hợp với Organizations: deploy 1 lần tới root/OU, cover mọi account + region. Khi có account mới được add vào OU, stack tự deploy. Phương án còn lại đều thủ công, không scale.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A CloudFormation template needs to reference the VPC ID from another stack in the same account/region without using parameter passing.

The environment spans multiple accounts and Regions. The company wants a managed solution that limits blast radius and supports controlled rollback or remediation when a change fails.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Fn::GetAtt
Fn::ImportValue
Fn::Sub
Ref

questions.explanation: Fn::ImportValue đọc output đã export bằng Outputs.Export.Name trong stack khác. Đây là cross-stack reference chuẩn. Fn::GetAtt lấy attribute của resource trong cùng stack; Fn::Sub là string substitution; Ref tham chiếu parameter/resource trong cùng stack.

questions.question:
A team uses CloudFormation to manage infrastructure. They suspect an engineer manually modified an S3 bucket policy outside of CloudFormation.

The operations team needs a solution that can be applied consistently to new and existing resources. The solution must avoid per-server manual steps and must integrate with AWS management services.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Run CloudFormation drift detection on the stack
Use AWS Config rules to check the resource against the template
Compare CloudTrail logs against the template
Re-run UpdateStack with the same template

questions.explanation: CloudFormation Drift Detection so sánh trạng thái thực tế của resource với template, báo MODIFIED/DELETED/IN_SYNC. Có thể chạy ad-hoc hoặc định kỳ qua EventBridge. Config rules có thể bổ trợ nhưng không native cho drift; re-update sẽ overwrite (nguy hiểm).

questions.question:
A DevOps team uses AWS SAM to deploy serverless applications. They need to test the API Gateway + Lambda locally before deploying.

All infrastructure changes must be reproducible from approved templates or managed configuration documents. The platform team wants to reduce manual console changes and keep a clear audit trail.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: sam deploy --local
sam local start-api
sam validate --offline
sam package --dry-run

questions.explanation: sam local start-api spins up một local API Gateway emulator + invoke Lambda trong Docker container, dùng template.yaml local. Test endpoint qua localhost:3000. Các command khác không đúng mục đích.

questions.question:
A team wants application configuration (feature flags, tuning parameters) to be updated independently of code deployment, with safe rollout (canary) and automatic rollback if errors spike.

The environment spans multiple accounts and Regions. The company wants a managed solution that limits blast radius and supports controlled rollback or remediation when a change fails.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store
AWS Secrets Manager
AWS AppConfig
AWS Service Catalog

questions.explanation: AppConfig được thiết kế đúng cho use case feature flag / dynamic config: deployment strategy (linear, all-at-once, custom), validator (JSON schema, Lambda), monitor CloudWatch alarm để auto rollback. SSM Parameter Store chỉ là key-value store, không có deployment strategy. Secrets Manager cho secrets. Service Catalog cho self-service infra.

questions.question:
A company uses AWS CDK (TypeScript) to define infrastructure. The team needs to synthesize the templates and review CloudFormation changes before deployment.

The operations team needs a solution that can be applied consistently to new and existing resources. The solution must avoid per-server manual steps and must integrate with AWS management services.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: cdk init → cdk deploy → cdk diff
cdk synth → cdk diff → cdk deploy
cdk deploy → cdk synth → cdk diff
cdk bootstrap → cdk deploy → cdk synth

questions.explanation: cdk synth tạo CFN template từ TS code; cdk diff so sánh stack hiện tại với template mới (preview thay đổi); cdk deploy apply. cdk bootstrap chỉ chạy 1 lần để setup CDK staging resources, không phải step thường xuyên.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A CloudFormation stack creates an EC2 instance and a security group. When the stack is deleted, the security group fails to delete because another resource references it.

All infrastructure changes must be reproducible from approved templates or managed configuration documents. The platform team wants to reduce manual console changes and keep a clear audit trail.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Add DeletionPolicy: Retain on the security group resource
Enable termination protection on the stack
Use UpdateReplacePolicy: Delete on the security group
Add a CloudFormation Stack Policy denying Update:* on the resource

questions.explanation: Termination protection trên stack ngăn DELETE accidental — phải tắt protection trước khi delete. DeletionPolicy: Retain để lại resource nhưng stack vẫn delete được; UpdateReplacePolicy: Delete làm ngược lại; Stack Policy không chặn delete stack.

questions.question:
A team wants to deploy the same web application stack to multiple AWS accounts using CloudFormation StackSets.

The environment spans multiple accounts and Regions. The company wants a managed solution that limits blast radius and supports controlled rollback or remediation when a change fails.
Which combination of steps should a DevOps engineer take to meet these requirements? (Choose TWO.)

options.text: Service-managed permissions (integration with AWS Organizations)
Self-managed permissions with AWSCloudFormationStackSetAdministrationRole and AWSCloudFormationStackSetExecutionRole
IAM Identity Center permission sets
S3 bucket policy for cross-account template access
Use nested stacks in the management account to deploy resources directly into every target account.

questions.explanation: StackSets có 2 permission models: self-managed (tạo 2 role thủ công ở admin + target accounts) và service-managed (tự động qua Organizations trusted access). IAM Identity Center và S3 policy không liên quan.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A Step Functions workflow processes orders. One state calls a Lambda that may throw a temporary network error. The workflow should retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff.

The operations team needs a solution that can be applied consistently to new and existing resources. The solution must avoid per-server manual steps and must integrate with AWS management services.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Add a Retry field on the Task state with ErrorEquals, MaxAttempts: 3, IntervalSeconds, and BackoffRate
Wrap the Lambda call in a try/catch inside the Lambda code
Use a Wait state followed by a Choice state to loop
Configure CloudWatch alarm on Lambda errors and restart Step Functions

questions.explanation: Step Functions Task state có Retry field built-in: ErrorEquals (vd: Lambda.ServiceException, States.TaskFailed), IntervalSeconds, BackoffRate, MaxAttempts. Native, không cần code retry. Try/catch trong Lambda chỉ xử lý error nội bộ; Wait/Choice loop tự build phức tạp.

questions.question:
A company provides developers self-service ability to launch standardized EC2 environments with pre-approved AMIs and instance types, while enforcing tagging and budget limits.

All infrastructure changes must be reproducible from approved templates or managed configuration documents. The platform team wants to reduce manual console changes and keep a clear audit trail.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: AWS Systems Manager Automation
AWS Service Catalog
AWS Control Tower Account Factory
AWS Proton

questions.explanation: Service Catalog cung cấp portfolio + product (CFN template) với constraint (launch role, tag, budget) để dev self-service trong khuôn khổ. Proton dành cho platform engineering (template lifecycle); Control Tower Account Factory tạo account mới; SSM Automation là runbook tự động hóa.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A CloudFormation template has an EC2 resource. When the AMI ID changes, CloudFormation replaces the instance, causing downtime.

The environment spans multiple accounts and Regions. The company wants a managed solution that limits blast radius and supports controlled rollback or remediation when a change fails.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Add CreationPolicy with ResourceSignal to wait for application readiness
Add UpdatePolicy with AutoScalingRollingUpdate (only valid if behind an ASG)
Add DeletionPolicy: Snapshot to the EC2 resource
Use Fn::ForEach to deploy two instances

questions.explanation: UpdatePolicy chỉ hỗ trợ trên AWS::AutoScalingGroup, AWS::Lambda::Alias, AWS::ElasticLoadBalancing::LoadBalancer, AWS::ElastiCache::ReplicationGroup, AWS::OpenSearchService::Domain. Pattern chuẩn là EC2 trong ASG + UpdatePolicy AutoScalingRollingUpdate để rolling update. CreationPolicy chỉ chờ signal, không tránh downtime; DeletionPolicy không liên quan update.

questions.question:
A team uses CloudFormation custom resources backed by Lambda for tasks AWS doesn't natively support. The custom resource hangs at CREATE_IN_PROGRESS and never completes.

The operations team needs a solution that can be applied consistently to new and existing resources. The solution must avoid per-server manual steps and must integrate with AWS management services.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: The Lambda function did not send a response (success or failure) to the CloudFormation pre-signed S3 URL
The Lambda function exceeded its memory limit
The IAM role for CloudFormation does not allow lambda:InvokeFunction
The custom resource ServiceToken is incorrect

questions.explanation: Custom resource Lambda PHẢI gửi response (PUT request) tới pre-signed URL trong event để CFN tiếp tục. Nếu Lambda crash / timeout / quên gọi cfn-response, CFN chờ tới 1 giờ rồi fail. Memory limit gây Lambda crash nhưng cuối cùng error sẽ propagate. IAM sai sẽ fail ngay, không hang. ServiceToken sai cũng fail ngay.

questions.question:
A team uses CloudFormation nested stacks. A change to a parameter in the parent stack should trigger update of a nested stack.

All infrastructure changes must be reproducible from approved templates or managed configuration documents. The platform team wants to reduce manual console changes and keep a clear audit trail.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Manually update the nested stack template URL after each parent change
When the parent stack is updated, CloudFormation automatically propagates parameter changes to nested stacks via AWS::CloudFormation::Stack resource
Use Lambda triggered by CloudFormation events to update nested stacks
Nested stacks cannot receive updates; they must be deleted and recreated

questions.explanation: Nested stack được khai báo qua AWS::CloudFormation::Stack với TemplateURL + Parameters. Khi parent update, CFN tự update nested stack với param mới. Đây là cơ chế native của nested stack.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A web application must achieve an RTO of 5 minutes and RPO of 1 minute across regions. Cost is a secondary concern.

The architecture must tolerate infrastructure failures without relying on engineers to make ad hoc changes during an outage. The company prefers managed AWS resilience patterns.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Backup and Restore
Pilot Light
Warm Standby
Multi-Site Active-Active

questions.explanation: RTO 5 phút, RPO 1 phút là cực kỳ thấp → chỉ Multi-Site Active-Active (Hot Site) đáp ứng. Warm Standby có RTO ~10p; Pilot Light RTO 10p-1h; Backup/Restore RTO giờ-ngày. Multi-Site tốn kém nhất nhưng cost không phải concern.

questions.question:
An application uses Route 53 with two regional endpoints (us-east-1 primary, us-west-2 secondary). When the primary fails its health check, traffic must automatically failover to secondary.

The application has variable traffic and strict availability requirements. The DevOps engineer must choose a design that avoids single points of failure and supports predictable recovery.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Weighted
Latency
Failover
Multivalue Answer

questions.explanation: Failover routing policy với 2 record (PRIMARY + SECONDARY) + health check trên primary là pattern chuẩn cho active-passive DR. Khi health check fail, Route 53 trả về secondary. Weighted không có failover; Latency dựa trên RTT; Multivalue trả nhiều record (active-active đơn giản).

questions.question:
A team uses an Auto Scaling Group behind an ALB. During scale-in events, they need to drain connections gracefully and run a script to deregister the instance from an internal service registry before termination.

The workload serves production traffic and must continue operating during common failure scenarios. The company wants to balance recovery objectives, operational complexity, and cost.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Lifecycle hook with state TERMINATING:WAIT
Cooldown period extension
ALB deregistration delay only
EC2 user data script

questions.explanation: Lifecycle hook TERMINATING:WAIT giữ instance ở trạng thái Terminating:Wait, phát SQS/SNS notification để chạy automation (Lambda/SSM), sau đó complete-lifecycle-action để chuyển sang Terminating:Proceed. ALB deregistration delay chỉ drain TCP, không chạy script custom; cooldown không liên quan; user data chạy lúc launch, không lúc terminate.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An RDS MySQL instance is Multi-AZ. The primary AZ experiences a failure.

The architecture must tolerate infrastructure failures without relying on engineers to make ad hoc changes during an outage. The company prefers managed AWS resilience patterns.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Read traffic continues from the standby; the DNS endpoint changes
RDS automatically fails over to the standby in another AZ; the DNS endpoint remains the same and resolves to the new primary (typically within 60-120 seconds)
The application must manually update the connection string to the standby
The standby becomes a read replica

questions.explanation: Multi-AZ là synchronous standby, không phục vụ traffic. Khi primary fail, RDS update DNS endpoint trỏ vào standby — endpoint giữ nguyên. Failover thường 60-120s. Application phải có retry logic + connection pool refresh. Read Replica là khác với Multi-AZ standby.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A global SaaS app stores user data in DynamoDB. Users in EU and US need sub-100ms read latency from any region, and writes must replicate globally.

The application has variable traffic and strict availability requirements. The DevOps engineer must choose a design that avoids single points of failure and supports predictable recovery.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: DynamoDB Streams + Lambda to copy data
DynamoDB Global Tables (multi-region, multi-active)
DAX cluster in each region
Multi-Region read replicas via AWS DMS

questions.explanation: Global Tables là multi-region, multi-active replication native của DynamoDB — write ở bất kỳ region nào, replicate sang region khác trong giây. DAX chỉ cache trong 1 region; Streams + Lambda tự build replication rủi ro consistency; DMS không phù hợp cho NoSQL global.

questions.question:
A DevOps team wants to test the resilience of their EKS application by randomly terminating pods during business hours.

The workload serves production traffic and must continue operating during common failure scenarios. The company wants to balance recovery objectives, operational complexity, and cost.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: AWS Systems Manager Automation runbook
AWS Fault Injection Service (FIS)
AWS Resilience Hub
AWS Compute Optimizer

questions.explanation: AWS FIS (chaos engineering) hỗ trợ inject failure có kiểm soát: terminate EC2/pods, stress CPU, network latency, API throttle… kèm stop conditions (CloudWatch alarm) để dừng thí nghiệm an toàn. Resilience Hub đánh giá RTO/RPO design (không inject failure); SSM Automation là runbook chung; Compute Optimizer cho rightsizing.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An S3 bucket in us-east-1 hosts critical data. Compliance requires that within 15 minutes of any object change, an identical copy must exist in us-west-2.

The architecture must tolerate infrastructure failures without relying on engineers to make ad hoc changes during an outage. The company prefers managed AWS resilience patterns.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: S3 Cross-Region Replication (CRR) with default settings
S3 Cross-Region Replication with Replication Time Control (RTC)
S3 versioning + Lambda triggered copy
S3 Batch Replication scheduled every 15 minutes

questions.explanation: S3 RTC là SLA: 99.99% objects replicate trong 15 phút, có CloudWatch metric để monitor. CRR mặc định không có SLA timing. Lambda copy tự build, không có SLA contractually. Batch Replication cho one-time/historical, không real-time.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An EKS cluster runs stateless workloads with variable load. The team wants pods to scale based on application metrics (e.g., queue depth), and nodes to scale automatically as needed with cost optimization.

The application has variable traffic and strict availability requirements. The DevOps engineer must choose a design that avoids single points of failure and supports predictable recovery.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: HPA + Cluster Autoscaler
HPA with custom metrics from CloudWatch + Karpenter for node scaling
VPA + Spot Instances
Manual node group scaling + CronJob to adjust replicas

questions.explanation: HPA scale pod theo custom metric (queue depth từ CloudWatch via CloudWatch metrics adapter). Karpenter chọn instance type tối ưu (mix spot/on-demand, instance family) — nhanh và efficient hơn Cluster Autoscaler. VPA điều chỉnh resource request từng pod, không scale replica.

questions.question:
A team designs a multi-region active-passive architecture for an application using Aurora MySQL. The secondary region must be promotable within minutes if the primary fails.

The workload serves production traffic and must continue operating during common failure scenarios. The company wants to balance recovery objectives, operational complexity, and cost.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Aurora Read Replica in another region
Aurora Global Database with secondary region
AWS Database Migration Service (DMS) replication
Manual snapshot copy every hour

questions.explanation: Aurora Global Database: replicate sang region khác qua dedicated infra (RPO ~1s, RTO ~1 phút khi promote). Cross-region read replica truyền thống có lag cao hơn và promote chậm hơn. DMS không phải cho DR. Snapshot copy RPO ~1h, RTO hàng giờ.

questions.question:
An application uses an SQS queue feeding Lambda. Sometimes Lambda fails to process messages due to transient errors. Messages should retry, and after 5 failed attempts, move to a separate queue for inspection.

The architecture must tolerate infrastructure failures without relying on engineers to make ad hoc changes during an outage. The company prefers managed AWS resilience patterns.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Set Lambda DLQ (legacy) on the function
Configure a Dead Letter Queue on the source SQS queue with maxReceiveCount of 5, plus a redrive policy
Set SQS visibility timeout to 0
Use EventBridge instead of SQS

questions.explanation: SQS DLQ với maxReceiveCount=5 redrive sau 5 lần fail. Lambda DLQ (legacy) chỉ áp dụng async invoke, không cho source SQS. Visibility timeout 0 gây duplicate processing tức thì. EventBridge thay đổi architecture không cần thiết.

questions.question:
A team operates an application across 3 Availability Zones using an ALB. One AZ becomes unhealthy.

The application has variable traffic and strict availability requirements. The DevOps engineer must choose a design that avoids single points of failure and supports predictable recovery.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: ALB stops sending traffic to the unhealthy AZ; remaining instances in healthy AZs receive distributed traffic
ALB sends 33% of traffic to each AZ regardless of health
ALB requires manual reconfiguration to exclude the failed AZ
ALB redirects all traffic to a single AZ

questions.explanation: ALB có cross-zone load balancing bật mặc định (free) — phân phối đều trên tất cả targets healthy bất kể AZ. Khi target ở 1 AZ unhealthy (qua health check), ALB tự loại khỏi pool. Không cần can thiệp thủ công. NLB mặc định OFF cross-zone (có tính phí inter-AZ).

questions.question:
A DevOps team needs to centrally collect logs from 50 EC2 instances across multiple accounts into a single CloudWatch Logs destination for security analysis.

The operations team needs actionable telemetry that can support alarms, dashboards, and incident investigations. The solution must avoid one-off log parsing scripts where managed AWS observability features are available.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Install the CloudWatch agent on each instance and use a cross-account log destination + subscription filter
SSH into each instance daily and copy logs to S3
Use AWS Config to collect logs
Use AWS Backup to back up the logs directory

questions.explanation: CloudWatch agent stream logs lên CWL trong account local. Tạo destination (cross-account) trong security account, subscription filter trong account nguồn forward sang. Pattern centralized log management chuẩn. Các phương án khác không scale hoặc sai mục đích.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An engineer wants to query CloudWatch Logs to find all API errors in the last hour, grouped by errorCode, sorted by count.

The company operates workloads in multiple accounts and needs consistent monitoring, logging, and audit visibility. The solution must preserve enough context to diagnose production incidents.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: CloudWatch Metrics Math
CloudWatch Logs Insights with a stats query
Amazon Athena over S3 export
AWS X-Ray service map

questions.explanation: CloudWatch Logs Insights cung cấp query language SQL-like (filter, stats by, sort) trực tiếp trên log group, không cần export. Nhanh, native. Athena work nhưng cần export trước; Metrics Math là cho metric không phải log; X-Ray cho distributed trace.

questions.question:
A team must monitor the availability of their public website from multiple geographic locations and be alerted if response time exceeds 2 seconds or status is non-200.

The team wants near-real-time detection of operational issues while keeping false positives manageable. The solution must use the correct CloudWatch, CloudTrail, Config, or tracing capability for the signal.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: CloudWatch Synthetics canaries
AWS X-Ray sampling
CloudWatch RUM (Real User Monitoring)
AWS Config rules

questions.explanation: CloudWatch Synthetics canary chạy script (Puppeteer/Selenium) định kỳ từ nhiều region, đo response time + status. Tích hợp CloudWatch alarm. RUM thu thập từ user thực, không proactive. X-Ray là tracing. Config kiểm tra cấu hình tài nguyên.

questions.question:
An organization wants to ensure that all CloudTrail logs cannot be modified or deleted, even by root users with admin privileges, for compliance.

The operations team needs actionable telemetry that can support alarms, dashboards, and incident investigations. The solution must avoid one-off log parsing scripts where managed AWS observability features are available.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Enable CloudTrail log file validation only
Use S3 Object Lock in Compliance mode on the CloudTrail bucket with a retention period
Use S3 versioning alone
Encrypt logs with KMS

questions.explanation: S3 Object Lock Compliance mode: object không thể delete/overwrite kể cả root, trong khoảng thời gian retention. Đây là WORM (Write-Once-Read-Many) đáp ứng SEC 17a-4, FINRA. Log validation chỉ detect tampering. Versioning ngăn delete đơn lẻ nhưng admin có thể xóa version. KMS bảo mật nội dung không phải bất biến.

questions.question:
A DevOps team operates 200 EC2 instances. They need to ensure all instances have a specific patch installed, and they need a continuous compliance report.

The company operates workloads in multiple accounts and needs consistent monitoring, logging, and audit visibility. The solution must preserve enough context to diagnose production incidents.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: AWS Config managed rule + Systems Manager Patch Manager + Patch Compliance dashboard
CloudWatch metrics + custom dashboard
Manual SSH into each instance weekly
Inspector findings only

questions.explanation: Patch Manager (SSM) deploy patch theo baseline + maintenance window. AWS Config managed rule ec2-managedinstance-patch-compliance-status-check kiểm tra compliance, hiển thị trong Patch Compliance dashboard và Config dashboard. Inspector chỉ scan vulnerability không deploy patch.

questions.question:
An application generates custom business metrics (e.g., 'orders_per_minute') that need to be visible in CloudWatch dashboards.

The team wants near-real-time detection of operational issues while keeping false positives manageable. The solution must use the correct CloudWatch, CloudTrail, Config, or tracing capability for the signal.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Use the CloudWatch agent with metrics_collected configuration to publish custom metrics
Use PutMetricData API with embedded metric format (EMF) via CloudWatch Logs
Write metrics to S3, parse with Athena, and import to CloudWatch via Lambda
Email the metrics to AWS Support for ingestion

questions.explanation: Embedded Metric Format (EMF) là cách hiệu quả: app log structured JSON với metric definition, CloudWatch tự parse và tạo metric. Không cần gọi PutMetricData (rate limited, đồng bộ). CloudWatch agent cũng work nhưng cho system metric chính, EMF tốt hơn cho business metric custom.

questions.question:
A team needs to track who made changes to security groups in their AWS account and be notified within 5 minutes of any change.

The operations team needs actionable telemetry that can support alarms, dashboards, and incident investigations. The solution must avoid one-off log parsing scripts where managed AWS observability features are available.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: CloudTrail → EventBridge rule matching AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress/Egress → SNS topic
AWS Config snapshot every hour
GuardDuty findings
CloudWatch Logs subscription on VPC Flow Logs

questions.explanation: CloudTrail ghi mọi API call. EventBridge match event source aws.ec2 + eventName AuthorizeSecurityGroup* / RevokeSecurityGroup* → trigger SNS gần real-time (~minutes). Config snapshot quá chậm và không near-realtime. GuardDuty focus threat detection. VPC Flow Logs ghi traffic, không API change.

questions.question:
A team uses VPC Flow Logs to analyze network traffic patterns and detect anomalies. Where is the MOST cost-effective place to store and query these logs for ad-hoc analysis.

The company operates workloads in multiple accounts and needs consistent monitoring, logging, and audit visibility. The solution must preserve enough context to diagnose production incidents.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: CloudWatch Logs only
S3 in Parquet format, queried via Amazon Athena
DynamoDB
RDS PostgreSQL

questions.explanation: VPC Flow Logs có thể export trực tiếp tới S3 với Hive partitioning và Parquet format → Athena query nhanh, rẻ (pay per scan). CloudWatch Logs đắt cho volume lớn của flow logs. DynamoDB/RDS không phù hợp với log analytics scale.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A CloudWatch alarm is configured with 'Treat missing data as: missing'. Metric stops reporting data for an extended period.

The team wants near-real-time detection of operational issues while keeping false positives manageable. The solution must use the correct CloudWatch, CloudTrail, Config, or tracing capability for the signal.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: ALARM
OK
INSUFFICIENT_DATA
DISABLED

questions.explanation: Với 'missing' treatment, alarm sẽ chuyển sang INSUFFICIENT_DATA khi không có data point. Các option khác: 'breaching' = ALARM, 'notBreaching' = OK, 'ignore' = giữ state cũ. Quan trọng cho việc detect khi agent/metric stop reporting.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A multi-account organization needs centralized AWS Config compliance reporting across all accounts and regions.

The operations team needs actionable telemetry that can support alarms, dashboards, and incident investigations. The solution must avoid one-off log parsing scripts where managed AWS observability features are available.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: AWS Config aggregator with organization-wide source
AWS Trusted Advisor
AWS Systems Manager Inventory
AWS Security Hub

questions.explanation: AWS Config aggregator collect Config data từ nhiều accounts + regions vào 1 dashboard. Với Organizations integration, tự động bao quát mọi member account. Security Hub cũng aggregate findings nhưng aggregator là feature chuyên cho Config. Trusted Advisor cho best practice riêng lẻ; SSM Inventory cho EC2 inventory.

questions.question:
An application running on ECS Fargate logs to STDOUT. The team wants to send these logs to a centralized OpenSearch cluster for search and visualization.

The company operates workloads in multiple accounts and needs consistent monitoring, logging, and audit visibility. The solution must preserve enough context to diagnose production incidents.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Use awslogs driver and stream to CloudWatch Logs, then use a CloudWatch Logs subscription filter to a Lambda or Kinesis Data Firehose delivering to OpenSearch
Use the syslog driver pointing directly to OpenSearch
Mount an EFS volume on the container and write logs to a file
Use the json-file driver and SCP files to OpenSearch

questions.explanation: Fargate hỗ trợ awslogs (CloudWatch Logs) và awsfirelens (FluentBit). Pattern phổ biến: stream qua CWL → subscription filter → Kinesis Firehose → OpenSearch. Hoặc dùng awsfirelens với FluentBit output OpenSearch trực tiếp. Syslog/json-file driver không phù hợp Fargate.

questions.question:
A company needs to automatically remediate non-compliant S3 buckets that have public read access. The remediation must run within minutes of detection.

The operations team wants to reduce mean time to recovery by using native AWS event sources and runbooks. The solution must be idempotent and safe to run during repeated events.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: AWS Config rule s3-bucket-public-read-prohibited + SSM Automation document AWSConfigRemediation-RemoveS3BucketPublicAccess as remediation action
Daily Lambda function scanning all buckets
Trusted Advisor check + manual fix
GuardDuty finding + email alert only

questions.explanation: AWS Config rule detect, kèm Remediation Action bằng SSM Automation document tự apply fix (apply Block Public Access). Native, gần real-time, audit-trail. Lambda hàng ngày quá chậm; Trusted Advisor không auto-remediate; GuardDuty email cần action thủ công.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An EventBridge rule must trigger a Lambda function when an EC2 instance state changes to 'stopped' OR 'terminated'.

The incident response process must be automated where safe, but the company must preserve evidence and avoid repeated manual actions. The solution should route events to the right remediation or notification workflow.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: { "source": ["aws.ec2"], "detail-type": ["EC2 Instance State-change Notification"], "detail": { "state": ["stopped", "terminated"] } }
{ "source": ["aws.cloudtrail"], "detail": { "eventName": "StopInstance" } }
{ "detail-type": ["EC2 Instance State-change"] }
{ "source": ["aws.ec2"], "detail": { "state": "stopped|terminated" } }

questions.explanation: EventBridge pattern dùng JSON array để match nhiều giá trị (OR). source 'aws.ec2', detail-type chính xác 'EC2 Instance State-change Notification'. Option D sai vì regex string không phải array. Option B chỉ match 1 state qua CloudTrail event name (lossy).

questions.question:
A DevOps team needs to replay past events that triggered a Lambda function failure during a recent incident.

The application uses event-driven components and asynchronous processing. The team needs a design that handles retries, failures, and replay without silently losing events.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: EventBridge Schemas Registry
EventBridge Archive + Replay
EventBridge Scheduler
EventBridge Pipes

questions.explanation: EventBridge Archive lưu events theo pattern (toàn bộ hoặc filtered). Replay phát lại events tới target trong cửa sổ thời gian — đúng để debug/recover post-incident. Schemas Registry cho event schema; Scheduler cho cron; Pipes cho stream integration source-to-target.

questions.question:
An application uses Step Functions to orchestrate orders. One of the states calls an external API that occasionally returns 5xx. The workflow must retry with backoff, then catch error to a notification state.

The operations team wants to reduce mean time to recovery by using native AWS event sources and runbooks. The solution must be idempotent and safe to run during repeated events.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Add Retry with ErrorEquals: [States.TaskFailed] and BackoffRate; add Catch with ErrorEquals: [States.ALL] and Next: NotifyState
Only use Catch, retries are automatic
Use a Parallel state to retry
Use a Wait state followed by manual restart

questions.explanation: Task state hỗ trợ Retry (in-state retry với backoff) + Catch (transition sang state khác nếu retries exhausted). ErrorEquals: States.ALL bắt mọi error còn lại. Đây là native error handling, không cần Parallel/Wait workaround.

questions.question:
A team wants to automatically open a ticket in their incident management system whenever a CloudWatch alarm enters ALARM state.

The incident response process must be automated where safe, but the company must preserve evidence and avoid repeated manual actions. The solution should route events to the right remediation or notification workflow.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: CloudWatch Alarm → SNS topic → Lambda (calling ticket API) with SQS DLQ
CloudWatch Alarm → direct webhook to the ticket system
CloudWatch Alarm → EC2 instance running a polling script
Email subscription on the alarm

questions.explanation: SNS topic + Lambda subscriber với SQS DLQ cho retry/fault tolerance. Decoupled (nhiều subscriber có thể nghe), scalable (Lambda concurrency). Direct webhook không retry; EC2 polling không near-realtime; email cần thao tác thủ công.

questions.question:
A DevOps team runs a CodePipeline that deploys to production. They want to be paged immediately if any pipeline execution fails.

The application uses event-driven components and asynchronous processing. The team needs a design that handles retries, failures, and replay without silently losing events.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: EventBridge rule on source aws.codepipeline, detail-type 'CodePipeline Pipeline Execution State Change' with detail.state FAILED → SNS topic with on-call subscription
Manual review of pipeline history
Use AWS Chatbot only without EventBridge
Configure CloudWatch metric filter on CodePipeline logs

questions.explanation: CodePipeline emit events tới EventBridge. Rule match state=FAILED + target SNS → PagerDuty/Opsgenie integration. Chatbot không thay thế trigger source. Metric filter trên logs phức tạp và lag; manual không scalable.

questions.question:
An organization wants to detect when AWS credentials are exposed (e.g., in a public GitHub repo) and automatically rotate them.

The operations team wants to reduce mean time to recovery by using native AWS event sources and runbooks. The solution must be idempotent and safe to run during repeated events.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: AWS automatically detects exposed access keys (via partner scanning), emits notification → EventBridge → Lambda calls IAM:DeleteAccessKey/CreateAccessKey + IAM:DetachUserPolicy as needed
Manually scan GitHub repos every day
Rely on GuardDuty CredentialAccess finding only
Use S3 inventory

questions.explanation: AWS có quy trình tự detect khóa lộ qua hợp tác với GitHub/Bitbucket/etc. — tự apply quarantine policy (AWSCompromisedKeyQuarantine) + gửi support email + emit event. Pattern khuyến nghị: EventBridge rule trên 'AWS Health Event' / abuse notice → Lambda revoke key + tạo key mới + notify owner. GuardDuty CredentialAccess detect anomalous use không phải repo leak.

questions.question:
A team runs scheduled jobs using EventBridge Scheduler. Some jobs are critical and must run exactly at the scheduled time across regions.

The incident response process must be automated where safe, but the company must preserve evidence and avoid repeated manual actions. The solution should route events to the right remediation or notification workflow.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Use EventBridge Scheduler with flexible time window of 0
Use EventBridge Scheduler with retry policy = 0 and exact schedule expression
Use Lambda with a CloudWatch Events legacy rule
Run a CRON job on an EC2 instance

questions.explanation: EventBridge Scheduler hỗ trợ cron, rate, one-time. Flexible time window cho phép spread invocation (giảm thundering herd) — nếu cần đúng giờ, set window=0 hoặc off và retry policy phù hợp. EC2 cron là anti-pattern (SPOF). CloudWatch Events legacy đang được replace bởi EventBridge Scheduler.

questions.question:
An application logs application errors to CloudWatch Logs. The team wants to receive a notification when the error rate exceeds 10 errors per minute.

The application uses event-driven components and asynchronous processing. The team needs a design that handles retries, failures, and replay without silently losing events.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Create a metric filter on the log group counting 'ERROR' occurrences, then a CloudWatch alarm on the resulting metric
Use AWS X-Ray sampling rules
Use a Lambda to scan logs every minute
Use Athena scheduled query

questions.explanation: Metric filter parse pattern (vd: [ERROR]) trong log group, count → CloudWatch custom metric. Alarm threshold > 10 / 1 minute → SNS. Pattern native, không cần code. Lambda scan không scalable; X-Ray cho trace; Athena cho ad-hoc, không streaming alarm.

questions.question:
A team uses AWS Systems Manager Automation to remediate issues. They want one runbook to be triggered automatically when an AWS Config rule reports non-compliant.

The operations team wants to reduce mean time to recovery by using native AWS event sources and runbooks. The solution must be idempotent and safe to run during repeated events.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: In the AWS Config rule, set a Remediation Action pointing to the SSM Automation document with parameters and optional Auto-remediation toggle
Use a CloudWatch dashboard widget
Hard-code the rule's evaluation Lambda to call SSM
Create a Step Functions wrapper

questions.explanation: AWS Config Remediation Action native cho phép chỉ định SSM Automation document, map parameters từ resource non-compliant, optional auto-remediate (no manual approval). Đây là pattern khuyến nghị. Các option khác phức tạp/không native.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An incident response process requires capturing a forensic snapshot of an EC2 instance's EBS volume and isolating the instance from network.

The incident response process must be automated where safe, but the company must preserve evidence and avoid repeated manual actions. The solution should route events to the right remediation or notification workflow.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Run an SSM Automation runbook that creates EBS snapshot, modifies security group to a 'quarantine' SG with no rules, detaches IAM role
Manually copy disk contents via SSH
Use AWS Backup with cross-region copy
Disable the AZ

questions.explanation: SSM Automation runbook orchestrate API calls: CreateSnapshot, ModifyInstanceAttribute (set quarantine SG), DisassociateIamInstanceProfile. Runbook có thể được trigger từ EventBridge (GuardDuty finding). Manual SSH chậm và phá vỡ evidence; AWS Backup không cho isolate; disable AZ ảnh hưởng workload khác.

questions.question:
An organization uses AWS Organizations with multiple OUs. They need to prevent ALL accounts in a 'Sandbox' OU from creating resources in regions other than us-east-1 and us-west-2, regardless of IAM policies in member accounts.

The organization has compliance requirements for encryption, access control, and continuous detection. The DevOps engineer must choose managed AWS security services where possible.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: IAM permission boundary on every user
Service Control Policy (SCP) attached to the Sandbox OU with aws:RequestedRegion condition
AWS Config rule
Resource-based policy on each resource

questions.explanation: SCP áp dụng guardrail toàn account/OU — không IAM nào trong member có thể vượt qua. Sử dụng condition aws:RequestedRegion NotIn [us-east-1, us-west-2] với Deny effect. Permission boundary chỉ cho IAM principal (không cover root); Config detect không enforce; resource policy không control resource creation.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A microservice needs to retrieve a database password securely. The password must rotate automatically every 30 days.

The environment includes multiple workload accounts. The solution must enforce guardrails consistently while preserving evidence for investigations and audit reviews.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: AWS Secrets Manager with automatic rotation Lambda
SSM Parameter Store SecureString with manual rotation
Store password in a CloudFormation parameter with NoEcho
Encrypt password in S3 and read at runtime

questions.explanation: Secrets Manager có managed rotation: tích hợp native cho RDS/Aurora/DocumentDB/Redshift, hoặc Lambda custom rotation cho khác. Parameter Store SecureString không có auto rotation. CloudFormation NoEcho chỉ ẩn hiển thị, không rotate. S3 không có rotation.

questions.question:
A DevOps team wants to detect Amazon S3 buckets containing sensitive data (PII, financial) across the organization.

The security team requires least privilege, centralized visibility, and auditable controls across accounts. The solution must not rely on shared long-term credentials or public access.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Amazon GuardDuty
Amazon Macie
AWS Inspector
AWS Security Hub

questions.explanation: Amazon Macie sử dụng ML để discover, classify, protect sensitive data trong S3 (PII, PHI, secrets). GuardDuty cho threat detection (anomalous behavior, malicious IP). Inspector cho EC2/ECR vulnerability scan. Security Hub aggregate findings.

questions.question:
An application running on EC2 needs to call S3 API. The team must avoid storing long-term credentials on the instance.

The organization has compliance requirements for encryption, access control, and continuous detection. The DevOps engineer must choose managed AWS security services where possible.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Store IAM user access keys in /etc/aws/credentials
Attach an IAM role to the EC2 instance via instance profile
Pass credentials via Lambda environment variables
Use SSH keys

questions.explanation: Instance profile + IAM role → SDK tự lấy temporary credentials qua IMDSv2 (token-based). Không có long-term credential stored. Đây là pattern bắt buộc của best practice. Store access key trên disk là anti-pattern. Lambda env không liên quan EC2. SSH key cho admin access, không cho API call.

questions.question:
A company uses AWS Organizations. They want to set up a new account for a project with pre-configured guardrails, networking, and logging.

The environment includes multiple workload accounts. The solution must enforce guardrails consistently while preserving evidence for investigations and audit reviews.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: AWS Control Tower Account Factory
Manually create account in Organizations console
AWS Service Catalog product
AWS Identity Center

questions.explanation: Control Tower Account Factory cung cấp account-vending workflow: tạo account mới qua Service Catalog product, áp dụng guardrails (SCP + Config), thiết lập VPC mặc định nếu cần, integrate centralized logging. Là cách chính thức để onboard account mới ở scale. Service Catalog đơn lẻ không có guardrail. Identity Center là SSO.

questions.question:
A company operates workloads across several AWS accounts. The security team wants to identify active threats in the accounts and detect software vulnerabilities on supported compute resources.

The security team requires least privilege, centralized visibility, and auditable controls across accounts. The solution must not rely on shared long-term credentials or public access.
Which combination of steps should a DevOps engineer take to meet these requirements? (Choose TWO.)

options.text: Amazon GuardDuty (threat detection)
Amazon Inspector (vulnerability assessment)
AWS Security Hub only, without enabling threat detection or vulnerability scanning services.
Amazon Macie discovery jobs only.
AWS Trusted Advisor checks only.

questions.explanation: GuardDuty phát hiện threat từ CloudTrail/VPC Flow Logs/DNS Logs (anomaly detection ML). Inspector scan vulnerability cho EC2, ECR images, Lambda functions. Athena query S3 (analytics), Glue là ETL — không phải security tool.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A developer needs to read a parameter from SSM Parameter Store from a Lambda function. The parameter is encrypted with a customer-managed KMS key.

The organization has compliance requirements for encryption, access control, and continuous detection. The DevOps engineer must choose managed AWS security services where possible.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: ssm:GetParameter only
ssm:GetParameter AND kms:Decrypt on the customer-managed key
ssm:* and kms:*
Only kms:Decrypt

questions.explanation: Để đọc SecureString, principal cần ssm:GetParameter (đọc parameter) VÀ kms:Decrypt (giải mã value) trên KMS key đã dùng để encrypt. Nếu là AWS managed key (aws/ssm), không cần kms:Decrypt riêng (AWS quản lý). Wildcard ssm:*/kms:* vi phạm least privilege.

questions.question:
An organization wants to centrally manage WAF rules across multiple accounts and ALBs/CloudFront distributions.

The environment includes multiple workload accounts. The solution must enforce guardrails consistently while preserving evidence for investigations and audit reviews.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: AWS Shield Advanced
AWS Firewall Manager
AWS Network Firewall
Amazon GuardDuty

questions.explanation: AWS Firewall Manager: central management cho WAF rule, Shield Advanced, Security Group, Network Firewall, DNS Firewall — trên nhiều account qua Organizations. Shield Advanced cho DDoS protection (per resource). Network Firewall là stateful firewall VPC. GuardDuty là threat detection.

questions.question:
A team wants to restrict S3 bucket access to requests originating from a specific VPC endpoint.

The security team requires least privilege, centralized visibility, and auditable controls across accounts. The solution must not rely on shared long-term credentials or public access.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: S3 bucket policy with aws:SourceVpce condition
Security group on the S3 bucket
IAM user policy with VPC condition
Block Public Access setting

questions.explanation: S3 bucket policy hỗ trợ condition aws:SourceVpce (VPC endpoint ID) hoặc aws:SourceVpc — chỉ allow request đi qua VPC endpoint cụ thể. S3 không có security group (chỉ EC2/ENI/RDS). Block Public Access là cho public, không phải VPC restriction.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A compliance auditor needs read-only access to all AWS Config compliance reports across all accounts in the organization.

The organization has compliance requirements for encryption, access control, and continuous detection. The DevOps engineer must choose managed AWS security services where possible.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Create IAM users in every account with AWSConfigUserAccess managed policy
Set up an AWS Config aggregator in a delegated administrator account + grant the auditor IAM role read access
Share CloudTrail logs with auditor via S3
Allow auditor root access to all accounts

questions.explanation: Config aggregator (delegated admin) tập trung dữ liệu compliance từ mọi account. Grant auditor role read-only trên aggregator. Single pane of glass. Tạo user trong mọi account không scale. Root access vi phạm bảo mật.

questions.question:
An application uses IAM role A in account 111 to access an S3 bucket in account 222. The bucket policy allows account 111. Suddenly, calls fail with AccessDenied. The team finds that the role's IAM policy was tightened.

The environment includes multiple workload accounts. The solution must enforce guardrails consistently while preserving evidence for investigations and audit reviews.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Only the IAM role's identity-based policy must allow s3:GetObject on the bucket
BOTH the IAM role's identity policy AND the bucket resource policy must allow the action
Only the bucket resource policy must allow the role
Use S3 ACLs instead of policies

questions.explanation: Cross-account access yêu cầu cả hai: principal's identity policy allow s3 action VÀ resource policy của bucket allow principal đó. Cả hai cùng tồn tại → access được. Cùng explicit Deny ở bất kỳ đâu → block. ACL là legacy, không khuyến nghị.

questions.question:
A team wants to enforce that any IAM role created in member accounts cannot grant administrative privileges (e.g., iam:*, *:*).

The security team requires least privilege, centralized visibility, and auditable controls across accounts. The solution must not rely on shared long-term credentials or public access.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: IAM permission boundary attached to every IAM role at creation time, enforced via SCP requiring permission boundary on iam:CreateRole
AWS Config rule that detects after the fact
GuardDuty finding
Send email to administrators when admin role is created

questions.explanation: Permission boundary là maximum permission cho IAM principal. SCP có thể require iam:CreateRole/CreateUser phải đi kèm condition iam:PermissionsBoundary = arn của boundary policy đã định. Boundary policy không cho iam:* hoặc *:* → mọi role mới đều bị hạn chế. Config rule detect sau khi tạo (không enforce). Các phương án khác chỉ alert.

questions.question:
A company runs CodePipeline in Account A and deploys CloudFormation stacks to production Account B. Artifacts are stored in an S3 bucket in Account A encrypted with a customer-managed KMS key.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Make the S3 bucket public-read so Account B can download
Account B's CloudFormation role must have permissions on the S3 bucket and the KMS key; Account A's bucket policy and KMS key policy must allow Account B
Copy artifacts manually to a bucket in Account B before the deploy stage
Disable encryption on the artifact bucket

questions.explanation: Cross-account CodePipeline cần policy bidirectional: bucket policy + KMS key policy ở Account A cho phép Account B s3:GetObject + kms:Decrypt; IAM role ở Account B cho phép tương ứng. Đây là 4 policy cần align. Public-read và disable encryption là anti-pattern bảo mật.

questions.question:
A DevOps engineer wants to enable CodeBuild to run multiple build configurations in parallel (e.g., Node.js 18, Node.js 20, multiple OS) from a single buildspec.yaml.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: CodeBuild batch builds with build-matrix or build-list
Multiple CodePipeline pipelines, one per matrix
AWS Step Functions Parallel state invoking CodeBuild
Lambda functions invoking CodeBuild API in a loop

questions.explanation: CodeBuild batch builds hỗ trợ build-matrix (combination matrix) hoặc build-list (explicit list) trong buildspec, chạy song song và aggregate kết quả. Native, không cần orchestration bên ngoài. Step Functions/Lambda là overkill.

questions.question:
A team wants to use GitHub Actions to deploy to AWS without storing long-term IAM access keys as GitHub secrets.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Store IAM user access keys in GitHub Actions secrets and rotate weekly
Use AWS IAM OIDC identity provider with GitHub Actions OIDC tokens and a role with web identity trust
Use SSH key authentication to a bastion host
Embed credentials in the workflow file directly

questions.explanation: AWS IAM hỗ trợ OIDC provider cho GitHub Actions. GitHub issues short-lived OIDC token, AWS verify và issue STS credentials qua AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity. Không cần long-term key. Đây là pattern chuẩn hiện nay (2023+).

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An ECR repository stores hundreds of images. The team wants to automatically delete untagged images older than 14 days and keep only the latest 30 tagged images.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: ECR lifecycle policy with multiple rules sorted by priority
Lambda function running daily
S3 lifecycle policy on the ECR backing bucket
AWS Backup retention

questions.explanation: ECR lifecycle policies cho phép định nghĩa multiple rules với priority. Rule 1: tagStatus=UNTAGGED, sinceImagePushed=14 days; Rule 2: tagStatus=TAGGED, countType=imageCountMoreThan, countNumber=30. ECR tự động dọn dẹp. Native, free, không cần Lambda.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A Lambda function is deployed via SAM. The team wants the deployment to run a validation Lambda BEFORE shifting any traffic, AND a smoke test Lambda AFTER full traffic shift completes.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: BeforeAllowTraffic for validation, AfterAllowTraffic for smoke test
ApplicationStart for validation, ValidateService for smoke test
Install for validation, ApplicationStop for smoke test
All in BeforeInstall hook

questions.explanation: Cho Lambda deployment, AppSpec có 2 hooks: BeforeAllowTraffic (chạy trước khi shift, nếu fail → cancel deployment) và AfterAllowTraffic (chạy sau khi shift hoàn tất, nếu fail → trigger rollback). Các hook khác (ApplicationStart, ValidateService) là cho EC2.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A CodeBuild project's buildspec runs unit tests and generates a JUnit XML report. The team wants this report visible in the CodeBuild console with test result trends.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Use the reports section in buildspec.yaml with a report group of type TEST
Manually upload the XML to S3 after each build
Use CloudWatch custom metrics
Email the report to admins

questions.explanation: CodeBuild Reports support test reports (JUnit, TestNG, Cucumber, etc.) và code coverage reports (JaCoCo, Clover, Cobertura, SimpleCov). Khai báo trong buildspec.yaml.reports với file-format và files. Console hiển thị pass rate, duration, trends qua time.

questions.question:
A company uses CodePipeline V2 with multiple sources. They want to trigger a pipeline only when changes are pushed to specific file paths (e.g., src/**).

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Pipeline trigger filters with file paths (Git push trigger)
Lambda invoke action filtering changes
S3 event notification with prefix filter
Pipeline cannot filter by file path

questions.explanation: CodePipeline V2 introduce trigger filters: theo branch, tag, file path patterns. Khi push không match filter, pipeline không trigger. Đáp ứng pattern monorepo (chỉ chạy pipeline khi thư mục liên quan thay đổi).

questions.question:
A company deploys Lambda functions by using AWS CodeDeploy. The release team wants deployment configurations that can shift traffic gradually between Lambda versions.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which combination of steps should a DevOps engineer take to meet these requirements? (Choose TWO.)

options.text: Canary (e.g., CodeDeployDefault.LambdaCanary10Percent5Minutes)
Linear (e.g., CodeDeployDefault.LambdaLinear10PercentEvery1Minute)
Rolling with additional batch
Blue/Green with in-place
Configure CodeDeploy to skip lifecycle hooks on instances that already passed health checks.

questions.explanation: Lambda hỗ trợ Canary (shift X% rồi đợi N phút rồi 100%) và Linear (shift đều theo X% mỗi N phút) và AllAtOnce. Rolling/in-place là cho EC2. Blue/Green với in-place là contradiction.

questions.question:
A DevOps engineer notices that CodeBuild jobs are slow because npm install downloads packages from scratch every time.
