The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: CodeBuild cache mode: S3 cache or local DOCKER_LAYER/CUSTOM cache
Use a larger compute type
Pre-install all packages on the AMI
Build in CodeCommit instead

questions.explanation: CodeBuild cache: S3 cache (artifacts cached across builds) hoặc local cache (DOCKER_LAYER, SOURCE, CUSTOM) trong cùng host khi available. Khai báo trong project config + cache paths trong buildspec. Significant speedup cho dependency reinstall.

questions.question:
A team uses Elastic Beanstalk and wants to deploy a long-running background processing application that consumes from an SQS queue.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Web Server tier
Worker tier with SQS daemon
Both Web Server and Worker tier
Beanstalk doesn't support background workers

questions.explanation: Beanstalk Worker tier có SQS daemon đọc message từ queue và POST tới ứng dụng tại localhost. Phù hợp cho background processing dài. Web Server tier dành cho HTTP request/response. Worker tier không có public endpoint.

questions.question:
A team manages multiple production accounts and wants to centralize CI/CD: one pipeline that deploys to dev/staging/prod accounts.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Pipeline assumes a CrossAccountDeploymentRole in each target account; trust policy in target accounts allows the pipeline account
Pipeline uses root credentials of each target account
Replicate the pipeline to each account
Use AWS Backup to push artifacts

questions.explanation: Cross-account deployment pattern: pipeline trong shared services account, assume role trong dev/staging/prod để deploy. Role trong target accounts trust pipeline account, có inline permissions tương ứng. Root credentials nghiêm cấm theo best practice.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A developer needs to deploy an updated CloudFormation template that includes IAM role creation. The deploy fails with: 'Requires capabilities: [CAPABILITY_IAM]'.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Acknowledge IAM resource creation by passing CAPABILITY_IAM (or CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM if naming custom roles) during stack update
Remove IAM resources from the template
Run the template as root user
Convert IAM resources to SCPs

questions.explanation: CloudFormation yêu cầu user acknowledge khi template tạo/sửa IAM. CAPABILITY_IAM cho generic IAM resources; CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM khi có custom name (Name property). Đây là safety check tránh accidentally tạo privileged identities.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An AWS CodeArtifact repository serves Maven dependencies. The team wants to ensure that if a dependency is downloaded from upstream Maven Central, the same version is retained in CodeArtifact even after future upstream changes/removals.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Package retention: once ingested from upstream, the package is stored in CodeArtifact
Schedule a Lambda to back up to S3
Use ECR cross-region replication
Disable upstream entirely

questions.explanation: CodeArtifact tự retain package đã ingested. Khi developer pull artifact từ external (Maven Central/PyPI/npm), CodeArtifact lưu copy nội bộ. Subsequent pulls dùng cached copy, không phụ thuộc upstream availability. Phòng tránh leftpad scenario.

questions.question:
A team uses Amplify Hosting for a Next.js frontend application. They want preview deployments for every pull request automatically.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Enable pull request previews in Amplify Hosting branch settings
Manually run amplify push for each PR
Use CodePipeline with Amplify action
Run a Lambda to detect PRs

questions.explanation: AWS Amplify Hosting có 'Pull request previews' feature: với mỗi PR mở, tự tạo preview deployment với unique URL, tự destroy khi PR đóng. Khai báo qua branch settings hoặc amplify.yml. Native, zero-config infra.

questions.question:
A DevOps team uses CodePipeline to deploy a CloudFormation stack with custom Lambda Layer dependencies. The Lambda code is in source, but the layer takes 5 minutes to build. They want to skip rebuild if dependencies haven't changed.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Source cache + custom cache paths in buildspec
Disable build phase
Use a shared EBS volume
Force fresh build always

questions.explanation: CodeBuild custom cache paths trong buildspec.yaml.cache.paths cho phép list directory để cache giữa builds (vd: /root/.m2/**, node_modules/**). Combine với S3 cache mode → đáng kể giảm thời gian build cho dependency caching.

questions.question:
An organization wants to enforce that ALL CodeBuild projects have logs encrypted with a customer-managed KMS key.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: AWS Config managed rule + automated remediation via SSM Automation
Manual audit every quarter
GuardDuty findings
S3 bucket policy

questions.explanation: AWS Config rule (vd: codebuild-project-envvar-awscred-check, or custom Lambda rule kiểm tra encryptionDisabled) detect non-compliant. Pair với SSM Automation document remediation → tự enable encryption. Pattern chuẩn cho compliance enforcement at scale.

questions.question:
A team needs to invalidate CloudFront cache after each deployment of static assets to S3.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Add a CodeBuild post_build phase that calls aws cloudfront create-invalidation with --paths '/*'
Wait for natural cache expiration (24 hours)
Delete and recreate the distribution
Disable caching entirely

questions.explanation: Pattern phổ biến: sau khi sync S3, gọi cloudfront create-invalidation. Có thể dùng cloudfront-invalidation action trong CodePipeline (custom action) hoặc đơn giản aws CLI trong CodeBuild. Wait 24h là disruptive cho user.

questions.question:
A team uses CodeDeploy with an Auto Scaling Group. After a successful deployment, new EC2 instances launched by the ASG do NOT have the latest application code.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: ASG launch template/configuration uses an outdated AMI; CodeDeploy needs to be configured to deploy on launch
CodeDeploy doesn't support ASG
Health check failed
VPC endpoint missing

questions.explanation: CodeDeploy deploys lên instances EXISTING tại thời điểm deploy. New instances launched bởi ASG sau đó dùng AMI cũ. Solution: enable 'AutoScalingHook' (CodeDeploy lifecycle hook trong ASG) — CodeDeploy tự deploy latest revision khi instance mới launch. Hoặc bake AMI mới sau mỗi deploy.

questions.question:
A DevOps engineer wants to schedule a CodePipeline execution to run at 2 AM UTC daily (e.g., for nightly tests).

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Create an EventBridge scheduled rule (cron) targeting the pipeline
Lambda function with setTimeout
Manual trigger by an engineer at 2 AM
CodePipeline has built-in scheduler with cron syntax

questions.explanation: EventBridge scheduled rule với cron expression target CodePipeline (action: codepipeline:StartPipelineExecution). Pattern chuẩn. CodePipeline V2 cũng có schedule trigger native nhưng EventBridge phổ biến hơn cho cross-service orchestration.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A CodeDeploy in-place EC2 deployment is failing in the BeforeInstall hook because the user data script doesn't have permissions to download from a private S3 bucket.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Attach an IAM role to the EC2 instance with s3:GetObject permission on the bucket
Hard-code AWS access keys in the script
Make the bucket public
Run the script as root

questions.explanation: EC2 instance profile + IAM role là pattern chuẩn để cấp S3 access. CodeDeploy agent dùng credentials từ instance metadata. Hard-code keys vi phạm best practice. Public bucket nguy hiểm.

questions.question:
A team uses CodeCommit and wants to enforce that all commits to the 'main' branch have a Jira ticket ID in the commit message.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: CodeCommit pre-commit hook is not supported; use a Lambda triggered by CodeCommit pullRequestStatusChanged event to validate the PR title/commits
CodeCommit branch protection rules
GitHub Actions integration
Beanstalk webhook

questions.explanation: CodeCommit không có server-side pre-receive hooks. Pattern khuyến nghị: CodeCommit phát events qua EventBridge (referenceUpdated, pullRequestCreated, pullRequestStatusChanged). Lambda subscribe, validate commit message/PR meta, post comment hoặc block merge qua API.

questions.question:
An application running on Lambda has a deployment that uses an alias 'live' with weighted routing: version 5 = 90%, version 6 = 10%. The team wants to programmatically check if v6's error rate exceeds 1% and roll back.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: CodeDeploy + CloudWatch alarm linked to deployment group; CodeDeploy automatically rolls back if alarm fires during the deployment window
X-Ray traces only
Lambda destinations
API Gateway throttling

questions.explanation: CodeDeploy deployment group có 'alarms' setting: nếu CloudWatch alarm enter ALARM trong cửa sổ deployment, CodeDeploy tự rollback (shift 100% về version cũ). Đây là native canary safety pattern cho Lambda + ECS.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A monorepo has 5 microservices. The team wants only the changed service's pipeline to run on each commit.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: 5 separate CodePipelines, each with CodePipeline V2 trigger filters limited to that service's path (e.g., services/svc1/**)
One pipeline that builds all services every time
Use Lambda to detect changes and trigger pipelines
Use Jenkins instead

questions.explanation: CodePipeline V2 trigger filters hỗ trợ file path patterns. Mỗi service có pipeline riêng với filter ('services/svc1/**'). Khi push, chỉ pipeline tương ứng trigger. Native, không cần code orchestration. Pattern monorepo phổ biến.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A CodePipeline includes a CodeBuild action that needs to access secrets stored in AWS Secrets Manager.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Reference secrets in buildspec env.secrets-manager section; CodeBuild fetches them at runtime via the project's service role with secretsmanager:GetSecretValue permission
Hard-code secrets in buildspec.yaml
Store secrets in CloudFormation parameters
Pass secrets via S3 file

questions.explanation: Buildspec env.secrets-manager: { ENV_NAME: 'secret-name:json-key' } — CodeBuild tự resolve và expose as environment variable. Service role cần secretsmanager:GetSecretValue + kms:Decrypt. Tương tự env.parameter-store cho SSM. Native, không expose secret trong logs.

questions.question:
A team needs to publish CodeBuild build status (PASSED/FAILED) back to GitHub as a commit status check.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Configure 'Report build status' in CodeBuild source settings when source is GitHub; CodeBuild uses the GitHub PAT/OAuth connection to post status
Write a Lambda function to call GitHub API
GitHub webhooks alone are enough
CodeBuild cannot publish to GitHub

questions.explanation: CodeBuild project source config 'Report build status to source provider' (cho GitHub/GitHub Enterprise/Bitbucket) — CodeBuild tự gọi API tạo commit status. Required: source provider connection có scope phù hợp (status:write). Lambda là workaround không cần thiết.

questions.question:
A DevOps engineer creates an Elastic Beanstalk environment but needs to customize the underlying EC2 (install agent, configure firewall rules).

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: .ebextensions config files in the source bundle (.config YAML/JSON for packages, commands, files, services)
Beanstalk doesn't allow customization
SSH into each instance manually
AWS Backup snapshot

questions.explanation: .ebextensions thư mục chứa .config file YAML khai báo packages, files, commands, container_commands, services. Beanstalk apply trong deployment workflow. Mạnh mẽ và versioned cùng source. AMI customization (Saved Configuration / Platform Hook) là alternative cho complex case.

questions.question:
A team uses CodePipeline for ECS deployments. They want a manual approval BETWEEN the build stage and the deploy stage, with a Slack notification when approval is needed. Best approach.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Manual Approval action + SNS topic in approval action; AWS Chatbot subscribed to SNS posts to Slack channel
Lambda function polling pipeline status every 5 minutes and emailing the team
Disable automatic transitions
Use Step Functions for approval

questions.explanation: Manual Approval action có optional 'SNS topic' field: khi action waiting, gửi message tới SNS. AWS Chatbot subscribe SNS, forward to Slack/MS Teams với clickable approve/reject. Lambda polling là anti-pattern.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An ECS Blue/Green deployment via CodeDeploy is configured with deployment config CodeDeployDefault.ECSAllAtOnce. The team realized 30 seconds after switch that the new version is broken.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Stop the deployment in CodeDeploy console; CodeDeploy reverts traffic to the previous task set immediately
Manually delete the new ECS task set via aws ecs delete-task-set
Update the ECS service to revert task definition
Restart the entire ECS cluster

questions.explanation: CodeDeploy ECS Blue/Green giữ 2 task sets song song trong deployment window (default 1 hour). 'Stop and roll back deployment' trong console gửi traffic ngay về task set cũ + xóa task set mới. Tốc độ chỉ giây.

questions.question:
A team builds a Docker image in CodeBuild and pushes to ECR. They want to sign images and have only signed images allowed to deploy via Amazon EKS.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: AWS Signer with Notation (OCI signature) + EKS admission controller validating signatures
ECR has built-in mandatory signing
Docker Hub Content Trust
AWS WAF rules

questions.explanation: AWS Signer (AWS-managed signing service) hỗ trợ container image signing với Notation (CNCF standard). EKS admission controller (vd: Sigstore policy-controller hoặc Kyverno) verify signature trước khi pull. Đây là supply-chain security pattern cho EKS.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A CodePipeline includes deployment to multiple regions. The team wants each region's deploy to wait for the previous region's deploy to succeed (sequential, not parallel).

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Place each region's deployment as a separate stage; stages execute sequentially by default
Use multiple pipelines and chain them with EventBridge
Set runOrder to the same value on each action
Pipelines cannot enforce sequential regional deploys

questions.explanation: CodePipeline stages chạy tuần tự. Mỗi region deploy là 1 stage → tự sequential. Trong cùng 1 stage, actions với runOrder khác nhau cũng sequential, cùng runOrder thì parallel. Vậy chia stages là đơn giản nhất cho regional progression.

questions.question:
An organization wants every CodeCommit push to trigger SonarQube static analysis BEFORE allowing merge to main. SonarQube runs on EC2.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: CodeCommit pullRequestCreated event → EventBridge → Lambda invokes CodeBuild project that runs sonar-scanner and posts comment to PR via CodeCommit API
Manual scan by developers
Use AWS Inspector instead of SonarQube
Schedule daily scan, ignore PRs

questions.explanation: Event-driven pattern: CodeCommit pullRequest events → EventBridge → Lambda/CodeBuild → run SAST → post PR comment (PostCommentForPullRequest API). Tích hợp với Approval Rules có thể block merge nếu scan fail. Pattern chuẩn cho shift-left security.

questions.question:
A company uses CodePipeline for production releases. The operations team wants to know which managed actions are available when a stage fails during a pipeline execution.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which combination of steps should a DevOps engineer take to meet these requirements? (Choose TWO.)

options.text: Automatically roll back to the most recent successful execution (if Stage rollback is enabled, V2)
Retry the failed stage manually or automatically via configuration
Automatically delete the pipeline
Page on-call via Slack with no other action
Export the production secret as a CodeBuild exported variable for later stages.

questions.explanation: CodePipeline V2 hỗ trợ Stage Rollback: tự rollback về execution thành công gần nhất khi stage fail. Retry stage: thủ công qua console/API hoặc tự động retry (configurable). Auto delete pipeline không phải feature.

questions.question:
A team uses AWS CDK Pipelines (Pipelines construct from aws-cdk-lib).

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Self-mutating pipeline: changes to the pipeline definition (in CDK code) are automatically applied to the pipeline itself on the next run
Free pricing
Faster builds
Supports Jenkins

questions.explanation: CDK Pipelines self-mutate: pipeline đầu tiên build chính nó, sau đó deploy app stages. Nếu CDK code update pipeline (thêm stage, action), commit tiếp theo sẽ apply self-update trước khi tiếp tục. Eliminate manual pipeline updates.

questions.question:
A DevOps engineer needs to roll back a Beanstalk environment to a previous application version.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: In Beanstalk console, deploy a previous application version from the version history
Restore from EBS snapshot
Re-upload the old code as a new version
Recreate the environment

questions.explanation: Beanstalk lưu application versions (default 1000 versions, configurable lifecycle policy). Console → Application versions → chọn version cũ → Deploy to environment. Nhanh và rollback granular. Không cần snapshot/recreate.

questions.question:
A company wants to validate that all CloudFormation stacks deployed to production accounts include specific tags (CostCenter, Owner) BEFORE the stack is created.

The operations team needs a solution that can be applied consistently to new and existing resources. The solution must avoid per-server manual steps and must integrate with AWS management services.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: CloudFormation Hooks (pre-provisioning) with a Lambda or AWS-managed hook checking required tags
AWS Config post-creation rule
Service Catalog only
Manual review every deployment

questions.explanation: CloudFormation Hooks chạy pre-provisioning (CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE), inspect resource properties trước khi apply. Custom hook hoặc AWS-managed (vd: AWSSamples::TagValidation). Có thể fail stack creation nếu vi phạm. AWS Config rule là post-creation (detect sau khi đã tạo).

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A platform team manages 30 microservice teams. They want to provide reusable, opinionated CloudFormation components (e.g., 'StandardVPC', 'EncryptedRDS') that teams can import via simple syntax.

All infrastructure changes must be reproducible from approved templates or managed configuration documents. The platform team wants to reduce manual console changes and keep a clear audit trail.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: CloudFormation Modules registered in CloudFormation Registry
Copy/paste templates across repos
Use CloudFormation Macros only
Service Catalog products

questions.explanation: CloudFormation Modules (CFN Registry) đóng gói nhiều resources thành 1 'logical resource' tái sử dụng. Teams reference Type: MyCompany::Network::VPC::MODULE. Versioned, governed. Service Catalog là cho self-service launching (khác level). Macros là template transformer thấp hơn.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A SAM project deploys a Lambda function with native binary dependencies (e.g., NumPy on Linux). On macOS dev machine, sam build produces incompatible artifacts.

The environment spans multiple accounts and Regions. The company wants a managed solution that limits blast radius and supports controlled rollback or remediation when a change fails.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Use sam build --use-container, which builds inside a Docker container matching Lambda runtime
Use sam deploy --force
Manually upload artifacts to S3
Compile on production EC2

questions.explanation: sam build --use-container chạy build trong Docker image phù hợp với Lambda runtime (vd: public.ecr.aws/sam/build-python3.11). Đảm bảo native binaries (NumPy, Pillow, cryptography) compile cho Amazon Linux 2/2023. Bypass platform mismatch.

questions.question:
A team uses AWS CDK and wants to enforce that all S3 buckets created across their CDK app have encryption enabled.

The operations team needs a solution that can be applied consistently to new and existing resources. The solution must avoid per-server manual steps and must integrate with AWS management services.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Use a CDK Aspect that visits every node in the construct tree and validates/annotates S3 buckets, throwing error if encryption is missing
Code review for every PR
AWS Config rule only
Disable S3 creation

questions.explanation: CDK Aspects implement IAspect, visit construct tree, can validate/modify nodes (vd: throw error if S3Bucket missing encryption, hoặc auto-set encryption = AES256). Chạy at synth time. Pattern enforcement-in-code, shift-left.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A Step Functions workflow processes a list of 10 million orders. Each item invokes a Lambda. The standard Map state has limitations on item count.

All infrastructure changes must be reproducible from approved templates or managed configuration documents. The platform team wants to reduce manual console changes and keep a clear audit trail.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Distributed Map state (introduced in 2022) processes large datasets from S3, can scale to millions of items with high parallelism
Standard Map state with maxConcurrency 1000
Multiple state machines chained
Step Functions doesn't support this; use Lambda directly

questions.explanation: Distributed Map state đọc input từ S3 objects/array, process tới 10K parallel, 1M+ items. Khác inline Map (limited inline, 40 concurrent). Phù hợp ETL, batch processing scale. Có ItemBatcher, ToleratedFailureCount để fault tolerance.

questions.question:
An organization manages 100s of SSM Parameters. They want hierarchical organization (/app/env/version) and the ability to retrieve all parameters under a prefix with a single API call.

The environment spans multiple accounts and Regions. The company wants a managed solution that limits blast radius and supports controlled rollback or remediation when a change fails.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Hierarchical parameter names with GetParametersByPath API
Use Secrets Manager instead
Store in DynamoDB
Use environment variables on EC2 only

questions.explanation: Parameter Store names có thể hierarchical (slash-separated, vd: /myapp/prod/db/host). GetParametersByPath với Path=/myapp/prod/ trả về tất cả params dưới prefix. Recursive flag để deep. Pattern config management chuẩn cho multi-env apps.

questions.question:
A team uses AppConfig for feature flags. They deploy a flag change with strategy AppConfig.Linear50PercentEvery30Seconds. CloudWatch alarm 'HighErrorRate' enters ALARM state during deployment.

The operations team needs a solution that can be applied consistently to new and existing resources. The solution must avoid per-server manual steps and must integrate with AWS management services.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: If the deployment has the alarm configured as a monitor, AppConfig automatically rolls back the configuration
Deployment continues regardless
User must manually rollback
AppConfig deletes the application

questions.explanation: AppConfig Deployment có optional 'monitor' alarms. Khi any alarm fire trong deployment window, AppConfig auto-rollback (revert config). Đây là 'safe deployment' pattern cho dynamic config tương tự CodeDeploy alarm rollback.

questions.question:
A team wants to ensure that all EC2 instances in their account have a specific CloudWatch agent configuration. They need the configuration to be applied at launch AND continuously enforced (drift remediation).

All infrastructure changes must be reproducible from approved templates or managed configuration documents. The platform team wants to reduce manual console changes and keep a clear audit trail.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: SSM State Manager Association targeting EC2 by tag, running an SSM Document on a schedule
EC2 User Data only
AWS Config
Lambda triggered by EC2 launch events

questions.explanation: SSM State Manager Association apply Document tới instances theo tag/instance IDs trên schedule (vd: rate(30 minutes)). Reapply liên tục → drift correction. User Data chỉ chạy 1 lần. Config chỉ detect không enforce.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A CloudFormation StackSet deploys a stack to 50 accounts. The team wants to roll out updates progressively: 5 accounts first, monitor for 1 hour, then continue.

The environment spans multiple accounts and Regions. The company wants a managed solution that limits blast radius and supports controlled rollback or remediation when a change fails.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Deployment targets with operation preferences (MaxConcurrentCount, FailureToleranceCount) + Stack deployment Hooks (CloudWatch alarm in account)
StackSet doesn't support progressive rollout
Manually split into multiple StackSets
Use Service Catalog

questions.explanation: StackSet operation preferences: MaxConcurrentCount=5 (chỉ 5 account cùng lúc), FailureToleranceCount=0 (stop nếu fail). Combine với CloudWatch alarms hoặc post-deploy validation Lambda để monitor giữa các batch. Native progressive rollout.

questions.question:
A company uses AWS Step Functions to orchestrate a deployment workflow. The workflow must handle errors in supported state types and route failures to cleanup logic.

The operations team needs a solution that can be applied consistently to new and existing resources. The solution must avoid per-server manual steps and must integrate with AWS management services.
Which combination of steps should a DevOps engineer take to meet these requirements? (Choose TWO.)

options.text: Task state
Parallel state
Wait state
Pass state
Choice state

questions.explanation: Task và Parallel states hỗ trợ Retry/Catch fields. Map state cũng có. Wait/Pass/Choice/Succeed/Fail không thực thi code nên không có error model. Retry chạy in-place, Catch transition tới state khác.

questions.question:
A team uses Service Catalog to provision standardized EC2 environments. They want developers (without IAM permissions to create EC2) to launch products via Service Catalog, but only with approved instance types.

All infrastructure changes must be reproducible from approved templates or managed configuration documents. The platform team wants to reduce manual console changes and keep a clear audit trail.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Launch constraint (specify role) + Template constraint (limit parameter values like InstanceType to allowed list)
Tag constraint only
Resource policy on EC2
Service Catalog doesn't enforce parameter values

questions.explanation: Launch constraint: Service Catalog assume role để launch product → user không cần direct EC2 permission. Template constraint: JSON rule giới hạn parameter values (vd: InstanceType must be in ['t3.micro', 't3.small']). Combine = guardrailed self-service.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A SAM template uses Globals section to define Lambda runtime once for all functions. A specific function needs Python 3.12 while Globals declare Python 3.11.

The environment spans multiple accounts and Regions. The company wants a managed solution that limits blast radius and supports controlled rollback or remediation when a change fails.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Function-level property OVERRIDES Globals; the specific function uses Python 3.12 while others inherit 3.11
Globals always wins
SAM throws an error
Both runtimes are used randomly

questions.explanation: SAM Globals là default cho tất cả resources cùng type. Function-level property override. Predictable behavior. Tương tự CloudFormation default vs explicit. Globals giảm boilerplate, override khi cần.

questions.question:
An organization migrating from OpsWorks Stacks (deprecated May 26, 2024) needs an alternative for configuration management with Chef cookbooks.

The operations team needs a solution that can be applied consistently to new and existing resources. The solution must avoid per-server manual steps and must integrate with AWS management services.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: AWS Systems Manager + custom SSM documents to install Chef Solo and run cookbooks; or migrate to a managed Chef SaaS like Chef Automate
Rewrite all cookbooks as CloudFormation templates
AWS OpsWorks for Chef Automate (managed)
EC2 user-data with bash scripts only

questions.explanation: OpsWorks Stacks EOL 2024-05-26. AWS đề xuất: (1) Systems Manager + SSM Document chạy Chef Solo/Zero (giữ lại cookbooks), hoặc (2) Chef Automate self-hosted hoặc (3) refactor sang IaC. OpsWorks for Chef Automate cũng đã EOL May 2024. SSM path = ít refactor nhất.

questions.question:
A team uses CloudFormation Transform Macros (AWS::Include, custom macros). The deployment fails with 'CAPABILITY_AUTO_EXPAND required'.

All infrastructure changes must be reproducible from approved templates or managed configuration documents. The platform team wants to reduce manual console changes and keep a clear audit trail.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: It acknowledges that the template uses macros that may transform the template at deploy time (could add resources not visible upfront)
It allows IAM creation
It bypasses drift detection
It enables nested stacks

questions.explanation: CAPABILITY_AUTO_EXPAND đảm bảo user nhận thức rằng template macro/SAM Transform/Include sẽ mở rộng template tại deploy time, có thể tạo resources không hiện rõ trong source template. Safety check tránh blind deploy.

questions.question:
A team uses AWS Proton to define standardized service templates (e.g., 'WebServiceWithRDS').

The environment spans multiple accounts and Regions. The company wants a managed solution that limits blast radius and supports controlled rollback or remediation when a change fails.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Proton manages full lifecycle: provisioning + ongoing maintenance/upgrades; environment vs service template separation
Proton is cheaper than Service Catalog
Proton supports only Terraform
Proton is for end users, not platform teams

questions.explanation: AWS Proton = platform engineering tool: separate environment (network, DBs) và service templates (app code). Platform team định nghĩa, dev team self-serve. Khác Service Catalog: Proton manage update propagation cross instances of same template. Service Catalog = catalog cho one-time launches.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A CloudFormation stack has a resource AWS::RDS::DBInstance. The team wants to delete the stack but keep the database for data retention.

The operations team needs a solution that can be applied consistently to new and existing resources. The solution must avoid per-server manual steps and must integrate with AWS management services.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Add DeletionPolicy: Retain (or Snapshot) to the DBInstance resource
Manually export the DB before deletion
Use UpdateReplacePolicy
Disable termination protection

questions.explanation: DeletionPolicy = Retain (giữ resource, hủy reference) hoặc Snapshot (tạo final snapshot trước khi xóa - dành cho RDS, EBS, ElastiCache). Default = Delete (xóa resource khi stack delete). UpdateReplacePolicy là cho update gây replacement (similar concept).

questions.question:
A team uses CDK to deploy stacks to multiple environments (dev, staging, prod). They want different config per environment without copy-paste of stack code.

All infrastructure changes must be reproducible from approved templates or managed configuration documents. The platform team wants to reduce manual console changes and keep a clear audit trail.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Use CDK context (cdk.json) or environment-specific Stage with props passed to a single Stack class
Maintain separate CDK apps per environment
Hard-code environment in stack
Use CloudFormation parameters only

questions.explanation: CDK Stages: group of stacks deployed together cho 1 env. Pass props ({ envName, dbInstanceClass }) tới Stack class. Hoặc cdk.json context để config khác nhau. Single source of truth + parameterized. Anti-pattern: copy CDK app cho mỗi env.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A platform team distributes AWS configurations via Systems Manager Parameter Store. They notice that frequent reads from Lambda are increasing costs and adding latency.

The environment spans multiple accounts and Regions. The company wants a managed solution that limits blast radius and supports controlled rollback or remediation when a change fails.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Use the Parameters and Secrets Lambda Extension to cache parameters/secrets in memory across invocations
Switch to Secrets Manager (more expensive)
Read parameters at deployment time and embed as env vars
Use DynamoDB

questions.explanation: AWS Lambda Extension 'Parameters and Secrets Lambda Extension' (Layer) cache values in-memory với configurable TTL. Lambda code gọi localhost HTTP thay vì SSM/Secrets Manager API. Giảm latency + API call cost đáng kể.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A CloudFormation template creates an S3 bucket with a randomly-generated name and an SNS topic that needs to know the bucket name.

The operations team needs a solution that can be applied consistently to new and existing resources. The solution must avoid per-server manual steps and must integrate with AWS management services.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Use Ref or Fn::GetAtt on the bucket resource in the SNS topic's properties; CloudFormation handles ordering automatically
Declare DependsOn explicitly
Create two separate stacks
Manually set the bucket name first

questions.explanation: CloudFormation tự tính dependency graph từ Ref/Fn::GetAtt/Fn::Sub references — order resources accordingly. DependsOn dùng khi không có data dependency nhưng cần explicit ordering. Pattern chuẩn là Ref.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A CloudFormation stack update fails midway. The team wants to know exactly.

All infrastructure changes must be reproducible from approved templates or managed configuration documents. The platform team wants to reduce manual console changes and keep a clear audit trail.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Stack Events filtered by ResourceStatus CREATE_FAILED/UPDATE_FAILED, plus ResourceStatusReason
CloudTrail logs only
X-Ray traces
Manually retry until clear

questions.explanation: Stack Events show timeline với ResourceStatusReason cho FAILED events — rất verbose (vd: 'API rate limit exceeded', 'Property X must match pattern Y'). Filter trong console hoặc DescribeStackEvents CLI. Đây là first stop debug stack failure.

questions.question:
A team uses Systems Manager Automation runbooks for routine operations. They want to enforce that runbook executions require an approval step.

The environment spans multiple accounts and Regions. The company wants a managed solution that limits blast radius and supports controlled rollback or remediation when a change fails.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Use 'aws:approve' action inside the SSM Automation document with approver IAM principals
Wrap runbook in Step Functions with Manual Approval
Run only via console with confirmation
Disable automation entirely

questions.explanation: SSM Automation document hỗ trợ action 'aws:approve' — pause execution và yêu cầu approvers IAM principal approve qua console/CLI. Native, không cần Step Functions wrapper. Useful cho destructive operations runbook.

questions.question:
A team wants their CDK app to fail synth if any IAM policy in the generated CloudFormation contains 'Action: \"*\"' or 'Resource: \"*\"' (overly broad).

The operations team needs a solution that can be applied consistently to new and existing resources. The solution must avoid per-server manual steps and must integrate with AWS management services.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: CDK Aspects implementing IAspect that inspect IAM policy statements and call Annotations.of(node).addError() on violations
AWS Config rule
CloudFormation template validation manually
Service Control Policy

questions.explanation: CDK Aspects + Annotations.addError() fail synth khi vi phạm policy. Visit construct tree, inspect Policy statements, validate. Shift-left security: catch trước khi deploy. AWS Config / SCP detect/enforce sau khi tạo (defense in depth).

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A SAM application has a Lambda function and an API Gateway. The team wants local testing of the full API with hot-reload of code changes.

All infrastructure changes must be reproducible from approved templates or managed configuration documents. The platform team wants to reduce manual console changes and keep a clear audit trail.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: sam local start-api --warm-containers EAGER --hook-name nodejs
sam deploy --watch
sam local invoke only
Run AWS CLI commands manually

questions.explanation: sam local start-api emulate API Gateway + Lambda local. --warm-containers EAGER giữ container alive cho fast iteration. Combine với hot-reload (file watcher) hoặc CLI option per runtime. Best dev loop cho serverless local.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A CloudFormation stack creates an EC2 instance with UserData that installs software. The team wants the stack to NOT report CREATE_COMPLETE until the software is actually installed (not just instance running).

The environment spans multiple accounts and Regions. The company wants a managed solution that limits blast radius and supports controlled rollback or remediation when a change fails.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: CreationPolicy with ResourceSignal: count=1, Timeout=PT15M; UserData calls cfn-signal --exit-code 0 after install succeeds
DependsOn another resource
WaitCondition with manual signal
Polling Lambda

questions.explanation: CreationPolicy ResourceSignal pause CFN tới khi nhận N signals từ instance (cfn-signal helper script trong UserData). Timeout safety. Đảm bảo bootstrap hoàn tất trước khi mark CREATE_COMPLETE. Modern alternative to WaitCondition.

questions.question:
A team uses Terraform to manage AWS infrastructure but wants AWS-native drift detection alongside Terraform state.

The operations team needs a solution that can be applied consistently to new and existing resources. The solution must avoid per-server manual steps and must integrate with AWS management services.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: AWS Config (detect resource configuration changes outside of Terraform), combined with Terraform state-based detection (terraform plan)
CloudFormation drift detection (only works for CFN stacks)
AWS Backup
Trusted Advisor

questions.explanation: AWS Config track configuration history bất kể IaC tool. Setup recorder + delivery channel + rules để detect drift. Terraform plan detect difference giữa state file và actual. 2 tầng: Config = AWS-side, terraform plan = Terraform-side.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A CloudFormation StackSet update is partially failing — 3 of 30 accounts fail because of regional service limit. The team wants the operation to NOT abort but continue with the other accounts.

All infrastructure changes must be reproducible from approved templates or managed configuration documents. The platform team wants to reduce manual console changes and keep a clear audit trail.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Set FailureToleranceCount higher (e.g., 3 or use FailureTolerancePercentage)
Increase MaxConcurrentCount
Disable rollback
Recreate the StackSet

questions.explanation: FailureToleranceCount/Percentage: số/% account fail trước khi stop operation. Đặt 3 (hoặc cao hơn) để cho phép 3 fail mà vẫn tiếp tục với account khác. MaxConcurrentCount là throughput, không liên quan tolerance.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A mission-critical application uses Route 53 for DNS. During a regional outage, automatic DNS failover via health checks is too slow because TTL is 60 seconds and DNS caching. The team needs sub-30-second failover guarantee.

The architecture must tolerate infrastructure failures without relying on engineers to make ad hoc changes during an outage. The company prefers managed AWS resilience patterns.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Route 53 Application Recovery Controller (ARC) with routing controls — instant, deterministic traffic shifting independent of DNS TTL
Lower TTL to 1 second
Use latency-based routing instead
AWS Global Accelerator (different layer)

questions.explanation: Route 53 ARC routing controls thực hiện shift theo data plane riêng biệt (không phụ thuộc DNS TTL). On/Off switch determinate, propagation gần như tức thì. Phù hợp cho enterprise critical với strict RTO. TTL=1 vẫn bị resolver caching unpredictable.

questions.question:
An organization runs DR drills quarterly. They want a Route 53 ARC feature to verify that the secondary region is READY to take traffic (resources scaled, configs sync).

The application has variable traffic and strict availability requirements. The DevOps engineer must choose a design that avoids single points of failure and supports predictable recovery.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Readiness Checks in Route 53 ARC: define resource sets in both regions, ARC continuously audits parity (capacity, configs)
Manual checklist
CloudWatch alarms only
AWS Health Dashboard

questions.explanation: ARC Readiness Checks: define cell (set of resources per region), check resource type parity và quota. Reports differences (vd: standby region thiếu Lambda concurrency). Surface trouble TRƯỚC khi failover thật.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An ALB serves a stateful web application requiring session affinity. Sessions should last 24 hours for each user.

The workload serves production traffic and must continue operating during common failure scenarios. The company wants to balance recovery objectives, operational complexity, and cost.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Duration-based sticky sessions on the ALB target group with cookie expiration = 86400 seconds
Application-based stickiness with no expiration
Disable cross-zone load balancing
Use NLB instead

questions.explanation: ALB hỗ trợ 2 loại stickiness: Duration-based (cookie AWSALB) và Application-based (cookie tùy app, ALB pass through). Duration phù hợp cho HTTP, expiration configurable. App-based khi muốn app control session.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An Aurora Global Database has us-east-1 as primary and eu-west-1 as secondary. The team wants applications in eu-west-1 to write locally (low latency) without manual failover.

The architecture must tolerate infrastructure failures without relying on engineers to make ad hoc changes during an outage. The company prefers managed AWS resilience patterns.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Aurora Global Database write forwarding from secondary region: writes are forwarded to primary, reads stay local
Promote secondary to primary daily
Use DynamoDB Global Tables instead
Aurora doesn't support write forwarding

questions.explanation: Aurora Global Database write forwarding (cho MySQL-compatible & PostgreSQL-compatible): app trong secondary region gửi write tới local endpoint, Aurora tự forward về primary, read consistency configurable. Single-master design giữ nguyên.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A DynamoDB table experiences hot partition spikes. Reads on a specific PK return ProvisionedThroughputExceededException intermittently. Provisioned mode is in use.

The application has variable traffic and strict availability requirements. The DevOps engineer must choose a design that avoids single points of failure and supports predictable recovery.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: DynamoDB adaptive capacity automatically reallocates throughput to hot partitions (enabled by default in provisioned mode)
Manually split the partition
Switch to RDS
Disable encryption

questions.explanation: DynamoDB adaptive capacity (mặc định) re-distribute throughput tới hot partition trong giới hạn table-level capacity. Giải quyết phần lớn hot partition trong thực tế. Nếu vẫn nóng → cần redesign PK (high cardinality, shard). On-demand mode auto-scale càng linh hoạt.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A Lambda function processes events from an SQS queue. The team wants successful results published to an SNS topic and failures sent to a dedicated SQS queue for investigation.

The workload serves production traffic and must continue operating during common failure scenarios. The company wants to balance recovery objectives, operational complexity, and cost.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Lambda Destinations with onSuccess (SNS) and onFailure (SQS); cleaner than DLQ for async invocations
Lambda DLQ only
Wrap in try/catch and call SNS/SQS manually from code
Step Functions

questions.explanation: Lambda Destinations cho async invoke: configure target on success (SNS/SQS/Lambda/EventBridge) và on failure separately. Destinations capture full invocation context. DLQ legacy chỉ failure, không có success path. Tuy nhiên SQS event source là sync invoke — destinations không apply, dùng SQS DLQ với maxReceiveCount.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A Lambda function serves a customer-facing API. Cold start latency on the first invocation per minute is unacceptable (~3 seconds).

The architecture must tolerate infrastructure failures without relying on engineers to make ad hoc changes during an outage. The company prefers managed AWS resilience patterns.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Lambda Provisioned Concurrency, optionally with auto-scaling based on utilization
Reserved Concurrency
Increase memory allocation only
Use API Gateway caching

questions.explanation: Provisioned Concurrency keep N instances 'warm' — không cold start. Charge cho duration provisioned. Reserved Concurrency là rate limit (ngăn over-scale), không liên quan cold start. Memory chỉ giảm cold start phần nào. API Gateway cache là khác layer.

questions.question:
A team stores user-uploaded files in S3. They want clients globally to upload to the nearest endpoint and S3 to handle replication transparently.

The application has variable traffic and strict availability requirements. The DevOps engineer must choose a design that avoids single points of failure and supports predictable recovery.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: S3 Multi-Region Access Points: single global endpoint that routes to the nearest bucket with active-active replication
CloudFront with S3 origin
S3 Transfer Acceleration only
Manual upload to multiple buckets

questions.explanation: Multi-Region Access Points (MRAP) cung cấp single global endpoint, route request tới bucket gần nhất (latency-based) + failover khi region down. Combine với CRR active-active. CloudFront cache content nhưng không phải solution cho global write.

questions.question:
A company runs production workloads on AWS. AWS Backup vaults contain critical backups. The compliance team requires that no one (including root) can delete backups for 7 years.

The workload serves production traffic and must continue operating during common failure scenarios. The company wants to balance recovery objectives, operational complexity, and cost.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Vault Lock in Compliance mode with minimum/maximum retention period; locked vault is immutable for the duration
IAM policy deny
S3 bucket policy
MFA delete

questions.explanation: AWS Backup Vault Lock = WORM-like, Compliance mode locks even root from deleting/changing retention. Once locked + grace period passed (3 days), không thể unlock. Governance mode chỉ IAM được control. Phù hợp compliance SEC, FINRA, CFTC.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An EFS file system is mounted across many EC2 instances in 3 AZs. The team wants lowest cost for rarely-accessed files.

The architecture must tolerate infrastructure failures without relying on engineers to make ad hoc changes during an outage. The company prefers managed AWS resilience patterns.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: EFS Standard with Lifecycle Management to move files to IA (Infrequent Access) after N days unaccessed
Manually move files to S3
Disable encryption
EFS One Zone (lowest cost but loses Multi-AZ resilience)

questions.explanation: EFS Lifecycle policy auto-move file unaccessed sau N days (7-90) tới IA storage class — giảm ~85% cost cho cold data, read-back có small fee. EFS One Zone cũng rẻ hơn nhưng kém resilient (single AZ). Lifecycle là default optimization.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An EKS cluster has pods that need to access an S3 bucket. The team is evaluating IRSA (IAM Roles for Service Accounts) vs EKS Pod Identity.

The application has variable traffic and strict availability requirements. The DevOps engineer must choose a design that avoids single points of failure and supports predictable recovery.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: EKS Pod Identity uses an agent on each node (simpler, no OIDC provider per cluster); IRSA requires OIDC federation per cluster and JWT exchange
IRSA is deprecated
Pod Identity is less secure
They are identical

questions.explanation: EKS Pod Identity (2023+): EKS Pod Identity Agent DaemonSet trên mỗi node, không cần OIDC IdP per cluster. Đơn giản setup, reuse 1 role across clusters. IRSA dùng OIDC + JWT (chuẩn hơn cho cross-cluster identity), vẫn supported. Cả hai cùng tồn tại.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An EKS application needs to scale pods based on SQS queue depth. HPA on CPU/memory is not sufficient.

The workload serves production traffic and must continue operating during common failure scenarios. The company wants to balance recovery objectives, operational complexity, and cost.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: KEDA (Kubernetes Event-driven Autoscaling) with AWS SQS ScaledObject — scales based on queue length
Cluster Autoscaler scales pods
VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler)
Karpenter scales pods

questions.explanation: KEDA = event-driven autoscaler. Hỗ trợ 50+ scalers: SQS, Kafka, CloudWatch metrics, Prometheus, etc. Scale deployment dựa trên external metric. HPA scale theo built-in resource metrics. Karpenter scale NODES (compute), không phải pods.

questions.question:
An application uses CloudFront with multiple origins (primary S3 in us-east-1, secondary in us-west-2). If primary fails, CloudFront should automatically fall back to secondary.

The architecture must tolerate infrastructure failures without relying on engineers to make ad hoc changes during an outage. The company prefers managed AWS resilience patterns.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: CloudFront Origin Failover with Origin Group: primary + secondary origin, retry on specific status codes (404, 500, 502, 503, 504)
Lambda@Edge dynamic origin selection
Route 53 weighted DNS at the origin
Manual CloudFront update on failure

questions.explanation: CloudFront Origin Failover: Origin Group với primary + secondary. Configure retry on status codes (5xx, 404, 403). CloudFront auto retry secondary nếu primary trả error. Native, no Lambda@Edge needed for simple failover.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A DynamoDB table experiences unpredictable spikes from a marketing campaign. The team doesn't want to over-provision but needs to handle bursts without throttling.

The application has variable traffic and strict availability requirements. The DevOps engineer must choose a design that avoids single points of failure and supports predictable recovery.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: On-demand capacity mode (pay-per-request, instant scaling)
Provisioned with auto-scaling (delayed scale up)
Reserved capacity
DynamoDB Streams

questions.explanation: On-demand mode: no capacity planning, instant scaling, pay per actual request. Perfect cho unpredictable spike. Provisioned auto-scaling có lag (CloudWatch alarm → adjust → propagate). On-demand đắt hơn cho steady traffic, ngon cho burst.

questions.question:
A team uses an ALB with target group health checks. Health checks marked instances unhealthy too aggressively during heavy load.

The workload serves production traffic and must continue operating during common failure scenarios. The company wants to balance recovery objectives, operational complexity, and cost.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Increase HealthCheckIntervalSeconds, HealthyThresholdCount, UnhealthyThresholdCount; or adjust HealthCheckTimeoutSeconds
Disable health checks
Use NLB instead
Recreate ALB

questions.explanation: ALB target group health check params: Interval (5-300s), Timeout (2-120s), HealthyThresholdCount, UnhealthyThresholdCount. Tăng threshold/timeout giảm false positives khi load. Trade-off: longer detection time for true failure. Tune theo SLO + load characteristics.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An EKS cluster uses Cluster Autoscaler. The team migrated to Karpenter.

The architecture must tolerate infrastructure failures without relying on engineers to make ad hoc changes during an outage. The company prefers managed AWS resilience patterns.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Karpenter selects optimal instance type from many options (no pre-defined node groups), launches in seconds, supports spot/on-demand mix natively
Karpenter is cheaper to license (both are free)
Karpenter requires no IAM permissions
Karpenter replaces Kubernetes scheduler

questions.explanation: Karpenter dynamic instance selection từ instance families đáp ứng pod requirements (vs Cluster Autoscaler require pre-defined ASGs). Launch nhanh hơn, consolidation tốt hơn, native spot integration. Cả 2 free; Karpenter dùng IAM permissions để tạo EC2.

questions.question:
A company replicates S3 data cross-region for DR. They want replication to start ONLY for new objects going forward, not historical data.

The application has variable traffic and strict availability requirements. The DevOps engineer must choose a design that avoids single points of failure and supports predictable recovery.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: S3 Replication is default 'new objects only'; existing objects not replicated unless you use Batch Replication
Always replicates all objects including historical
Replication is impossible for new objects only
Manually copy objects

questions.explanation: S3 Replication (CRR/SRR) chỉ replicate objects MỚI sau khi rule được enabled. Existing objects: cần Batch Replication (one-time job để replicate historical). Default behavior phù hợp khi chỉ cần forward DR coverage.

questions.question:
A team designs a Multi-AZ application with stateless web tier + RDS Multi-AZ. They want chaos testing of AZ failure WITHOUT actually impacting production.

The workload serves production traffic and must continue operating during common failure scenarios. The company wants to balance recovery objectives, operational complexity, and cost.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: AWS Fault Injection Service (FIS) with aws:ec2:terminate-instances + AWS Resilience Hub for assessment
Manually terminate EC2 in console (risky)
CloudWatch alarms only
AWS Backup restore drill

questions.explanation: AWS FIS chaos engineering controlled: experiment template chọn targets (filter by tag/AZ), actions (terminate, throttle, stress), stop conditions (CloudWatch alarm → auto rollback). Resilience Hub assess design + RTO/RPO. Safe alternative to manual chaos.

questions.question:
An application uses DynamoDB DAX for read caching. Some queries return stale data.

The architecture must tolerate infrastructure failures without relying on engineers to make ad hoc changes during an outage. The company prefers managed AWS resilience patterns.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: DAX serves from item cache (TTL configurable) and query cache; eventual consistency reads can return stale data — use strongly consistent reads if needed (bypasses DAX cache)
DAX is broken
DynamoDB Streams are not enabled
Cross-region replication is delayed

questions.explanation: DAX cache (item + query) trả về eventual consistency data trong TTL window. Strongly consistent read bypass DAX, gọi thẳng DynamoDB. Design choice: DAX trade consistency cho latency. Tune TTL hoặc dùng strongly consistent khi cần real-time.

questions.question:
A team needs to replicate AWS Backup recovery points (e.g., EBS snapshots, RDS snapshots) to a different AWS account for an air-gapped DR scenario.

The application has variable traffic and strict availability requirements. The DevOps engineer must choose a design that avoids single points of failure and supports predictable recovery.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: AWS Backup cross-account copy via backup plans with destination vault in another account in the same Organization
AWS Backup doesn't support cross-account
Manual snapshot share
S3 replication

questions.explanation: AWS Backup cross-account backup (Organizations integration required): backup plan rule với copy action tới vault ở destination account. Encryption với KMS key shared. Pattern DR + compliance isolation. Cross-region cũng tương tự.

questions.question:
A team uses RDS Read Replicas to scale read traffic. Application reads occasionally return data older than 1 minute behind primary.

The workload serves production traffic and must continue operating during common failure scenarios. The company wants to balance recovery objectives, operational complexity, and cost.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Read Replica is asynchronous; replica lag depends on workload. Use CloudWatch ReplicaLag metric to monitor; consider Aurora replicas (sub-second lag) for tighter consistency
Replica is broken
Multi-AZ standby is being read
DNS cache

questions.explanation: RDS Read Replicas async replication, lag từ giây đến phút phụ thuộc write throughput. Aurora replicas có sub-second lag (storage layer replication). Multi-AZ standby KHÔNG phục vụ traffic. Monitor ReplicaLag CloudWatch metric.

questions.question:
A team runs a stateful application on a single EC2 instance. They cannot easily refactor for HA but need rapid recovery.

The architecture must tolerate infrastructure failures without relying on engineers to make ad hoc changes during an outage. The company prefers managed AWS resilience patterns.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Put the instance in an Auto Scaling Group of min=1 max=1 across multiple subnets; ASG replaces failed instance automatically
Refactor to stateless first
Take daily snapshots manually
Move to Lambda

questions.explanation: ASG min=max=1 cross-subnet AZs: nếu instance fail health check, ASG terminate + launch mới trong AZ healthy. Combine với EBS snapshots + user data restore script. Single-instance HA pattern cho legacy app, không cần refactor.

questions.question:
A team needs an alarm that fires when the 5-minute average CPU is high AND the network throughput is also high (compound condition).

The team wants near-real-time detection of operational issues while keeping false positives manageable. The solution must use the correct CloudWatch, CloudTrail, Config, or tracing capability for the signal.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: CloudWatch Composite Alarm with rule expression ALARM(cpu_alarm) AND ALARM(network_alarm)
Metric Math only
Multiple SNS subscriptions
Lambda function evaluating multiple alarms manually

questions.explanation: Composite Alarms combine multiple alarms với boolean expression (AND, OR, NOT). Single notification khi compound state ALARM. Giảm noise của individual alarms, gửi actionable alert. Metric Math kết hợp metric data, không phải alarm state.

questions.question:
A team wants to detect anomalies in API request latency that don't fit a fixed threshold (e.g., baseline varies by hour).

The operations team needs actionable telemetry that can support alarms, dashboards, and incident investigations. The solution must avoid one-off log parsing scripts where managed AWS observability features are available.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: CloudWatch Anomaly Detection: ML model trained on metric history; alarm when metric deviates from expected band
Static threshold alarm
CloudWatch Logs Insights
Trusted Advisor

questions.explanation: CloudWatch Anomaly Detection xây dựng model học pattern hourly/daily/weekly. Alarm condition: metric outside band of N standard deviations. Tự thích nghi với baseline thay đổi. Phù hợp cho cyclical metrics (traffic giờ làm việc cao, đêm thấp).

questions.question:
A team wants to identify top contributors (e.g., IP addresses generating most traffic, users causing most errors) from CloudWatch Logs in near real-time.

The company operates workloads in multiple accounts and needs consistent monitoring, logging, and audit visibility. The solution must preserve enough context to diagnose production incidents.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: CloudWatch Contributor Insights: analyzes log streams in real-time, surfaces top-N contributors based on configurable rules
Logs Insights only
Athena scheduled query
Manual log inspection

questions.explanation: Contributor Insights real-time analytics trên logs/metric streams. Rule định nghĩa contributor (vd: từ JSON field clientIP), aggregator (Count, Sum). Trả về top-N. Logs Insights ad-hoc query, không real-time tracking.

questions.question:
A team uses X-Ray for distributed tracing. They want a unified view of services + traces + metrics + logs in CloudWatch.

The team wants near-real-time detection of operational issues while keeping false positives manageable. The solution must use the correct CloudWatch, CloudTrail, Config, or tracing capability for the signal.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: CloudWatch ServiceLens (X-Ray + CloudWatch integration showing service map with metrics + traces + logs)
CloudWatch Dashboards
AWS Health Dashboard
Trusted Advisor

questions.explanation: ServiceLens combine X-Ray service map + CloudWatch metrics/logs/alarms vào 1 view. Click node để thấy traces, error rate, latency, related logs. Single pane for observability. Yêu cầu X-Ray instrumentation + CloudWatch agent.

questions.question:
A team wants to monitor real user experience (browser performance, JS errors, page load time) on their web app.

The operations team needs actionable telemetry that can support alarms, dashboards, and incident investigations. The solution must avoid one-off log parsing scripts where managed AWS observability features are available.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: CloudWatch RUM (Real User Monitoring) — JS snippet on pages, collects real user metrics
CloudWatch Synthetics canaries (synthetic, not real user)
X-Ray sampling
VPC Flow Logs

questions.explanation: CloudWatch RUM: include lightweight JS snippet, collect Real User Metrics (Core Web Vitals, errors, sessions, page views). Bổ sung Synthetics (proactive, from synthetic locations). RUM cho insight production user thực.

questions.question:
An organization wants to query CloudTrail across all accounts and regions with SQL, store events for 7 years, and analyze long-term trends.

The company operates workloads in multiple accounts and needs consistent monitoring, logging, and audit visibility. The solution must preserve enough context to diagnose production incidents.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: CloudTrail Lake — managed data lake with SQL queries, configurable retention up to 7 years (or 10 with Lake)
Export CloudTrail logs to S3 and query with Athena
Push to OpenSearch
DynamoDB

questions.explanation: CloudTrail Lake (managed data lake) ingest events từ multi-region/multi-account Organization, retention lên 10 năm, SQL query trực tiếp. Khác CloudTrail trail truyền thống (90-day history + S3 export). Lake giảm setup ETL phức tạp.

questions.question:
A DevOps engineer wants to detect unusual API activity (e.g., spike in IAM CreateUser calls) and be alerted automatically.

The team wants near-real-time detection of operational issues while keeping false positives manageable. The solution must use the correct CloudWatch, CloudTrail, Config, or tracing capability for the signal.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: CloudTrail Insights detects abnormal management API activity using ML; emits Insight events to S3 and EventBridge
CloudTrail data events
GuardDuty IAM finding type only
Manual review

questions.explanation: CloudTrail Insights phân tích baseline API call volume + detect anomalies (vd: spike RunInstances bất thường). Emit Insight events. Subscribe qua EventBridge → SNS/Lambda. Bổ sung GuardDuty (rộng hơn về threat).

questions.question:
A team manages 50 accounts and wants to deploy a standardized AWS Config conformance pack (set of Config rules) across all accounts.

The operations team needs actionable telemetry that can support alarms, dashboards, and incident investigations. The solution must avoid one-off log parsing scripts where managed AWS observability features are available.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Deploy conformance pack from delegated administrator account targeting Organization, using AWS managed or custom rule template (YAML)
Manually create rules in each account
Use AWS Config aggregator only
Use AWS Backup

questions.explanation: Conformance pack = collection of Config rules + remediation actions trong YAML template. Deploy qua Organizations integration với delegated admin account → tự apply mọi account/region. Aggregator chỉ data, không apply rules.

questions.question:
A team needs to troubleshoot 'Why can't EC2 instance A reach RDS instance B' across complex VPCs.

The company operates workloads in multiple accounts and needs consistent monitoring, logging, and audit visibility. The solution must preserve enough context to diagnose production incidents.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: VPC Reachability Analyzer (source/destination, analyzes security groups, NACLs, route tables, returns path or blocking config)
VPC Flow Logs only
X-Ray traces
AWS Health

questions.explanation: Reachability Analyzer static analysis network path: SG rules, NACLs, route tables, peering, TGW. Trả về path nếu reachable hoặc resource block nếu không. Không gửi traffic thực. Khác Flow Logs (record actual flow).

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A security team wants to detect unintended network access paths (e.g., a public subnet inadvertently routing to a sensitive DB subnet) across all VPCs.

The team wants near-real-time detection of operational issues while keeping false positives manageable. The solution must use the correct CloudWatch, CloudTrail, Config, or tracing capability for the signal.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: VPC Network Access Analyzer — define network access scope, analyzer surfaces all paths matching the scope
GuardDuty findings
Flow Logs grep
Trusted Advisor

questions.explanation: Network Access Analyzer: define Network Access Scope (Source = Internet, Destination = SensitiveSubnet). Tool list mọi network path matching scope. Catch misconfiguration (publicly-routable to private DB) at scale.

questions.question:
A team operates on AWS and wants notifications for AWS-side events (planned maintenance, account-impacting issues).

The operations team needs actionable telemetry that can support alarms, dashboards, and incident investigations. The solution must avoid one-off log parsing scripts where managed AWS observability features are available.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: AWS Health API events → EventBridge → SNS / Slack / runbook automation
Email aws-support@
CloudWatch alarms only
RSS feed manual subscribe

questions.explanation: AWS Health Dashboard + Health API emit events tới EventBridge: scheduled maintenance, accountnotification, account issues, public outages. EventBridge → SNS/Lambda automated response. Pattern operational awareness chuẩn.

questions.question:
A team runs containers on EKS and wants per-container metrics (CPU, memory, network) + logs in CloudWatch with minimal setup.

The company operates workloads in multiple accounts and needs consistent monitoring, logging, and audit visibility. The solution must preserve enough context to diagnose production incidents.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: CloudWatch Observability EKS add-on (managed Fluent Bit + CloudWatch agent) — installs DaemonSets to ship metrics + logs
Manually install Datadog agent
CloudWatch Synthetics
X-Ray daemon

questions.explanation: Amazon CloudWatch Observability add-on cho EKS (managed by AWS): triển khai CloudWatch agent + Fluent Bit DaemonSet, Container Insights ON, application signals. One-click integration.

questions.question:
A team operates a Lambda-based application and wants distributed tracing across Lambda + DynamoDB + SNS.

The team wants near-real-time detection of operational issues while keeping false positives manageable. The solution must use the correct CloudWatch, CloudTrail, Config, or tracing capability for the signal.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: ADOT (AWS Distro for OpenTelemetry) — vendor-neutral, supports CloudWatch + X-Ray + other backends
X-Ray SDK only (still works, but ADOT is OTel-based future)
CloudWatch Logs only
Manual logging

questions.explanation: ADOT là AWS distribution của OpenTelemetry — vendor-neutral, future-proof. Export traces/metrics tới X-Ray, CloudWatch, hoặc third-party. AWS recommend ADOT cho new apps. X-Ray SDK vẫn supported, dùng cho existing.

questions.question:
A team needs to centralize CloudWatch Logs from 100+ accounts to a security account for long-term analysis.

The operations team needs actionable telemetry that can support alarms, dashboards, and incident investigations. The solution must avoid one-off log parsing scripts where managed AWS observability features are available.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Each source account creates a subscription filter forwarding logs to a Kinesis Data Stream / Firehose destination in the security account, then to S3 / OpenSearch
Manual export per account
Use VPC peering only
CloudWatch Logs doesn't support cross-account
