questions.explanation: CloudWatch Logs cross-account destinations: destination resource trong security account, source accounts attach subscription filter target destination. Forward sang Kinesis Stream/Firehose → S3/OpenSearch. Scalable cho 100+ accounts.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A CloudWatch Logs Insights query is needed to find all log entries containing 'OutOfMemoryError' in the last hour, count by service.

The company operates workloads in multiple accounts and needs consistent monitoring, logging, and audit visibility. The solution must preserve enough context to diagnose production incidents.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: filter @message like /OutOfMemoryError/ | stats count(*) by service
SELECT * FROM logs WHERE message='OOM'
JMESPath query
Regex only

questions.explanation: Logs Insights query language: filter (where), parse (extract field), stats (aggregate), sort. Có thể combine. Time range chọn ở UI. Trên scale Petabyte logs, query ngon hơn grep file thủ công.

questions.question:
A team wants to publish custom application metrics (e.g., 'cart_abandonment_rate') with high cardinality (per user, per region). High-frequency calls to PutMetricData are expensive and rate-limited.

The team wants near-real-time detection of operational issues while keeping false positives manageable. The solution must use the correct CloudWatch, CloudTrail, Config, or tracing capability for the signal.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Use Embedded Metric Format (EMF): write structured JSON logs with metric metadata; CloudWatch ingests as metrics from logs at no extra API cost
Use PutMetricData with batched 1000 metrics per call
Store metrics in S3 only
Use X-Ray segments

questions.explanation: EMF: log structured JSON với _aws.CloudWatchMetrics descriptor. CloudWatch tự ingest as metrics khi log. Async, không rate limited như PutMetricData. Lib: aws-embedded-metrics-* SDK. Phù hợp cho high cardinality.

questions.question:
A team uses CloudWatch Synthetics canaries to monitor a website. They want to capture screenshots on failure to debug.

The operations team needs actionable telemetry that can support alarms, dashboards, and incident investigations. The solution must avoid one-off log parsing scripts where managed AWS observability features are available.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Canary script uses synthetics.takeScreenshot() during workflow; screenshots stored in S3 + accessible from canary results
Enable AWS Config
CloudWatch Synthetics doesn't support screenshots
Use CloudFront only

questions.explanation: Synthetics canary script (Node.js Puppeteer / Selenium / Playwright) support takeScreenshot() — lưu vào canary's S3 artifact bucket. Pair với CloudWatch alarm khi canary fail → on-call có visual evidence.

questions.question:
A team wants real-time tail of CloudWatch Logs (similar to `tail -f`) without exporting.

The company operates workloads in multiple accounts and needs consistent monitoring, logging, and audit visibility. The solution must preserve enough context to diagnose production incidents.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: CloudWatch Logs Live Tail (real-time streaming of log events in the console)
Logs Insights query repeating every second
Export to S3 + tail S3 (no such API)
Lambda function polling

questions.explanation: Live Tail: real-time streaming via console (hoặc StartLiveTail API). Filter by log group, log stream, query terms. Pay per session minute. Phù hợp khi debug active issue.

questions.question:
A team wants to track AWS account spending against monthly budget and get alerts at 50% / 80% / 100% usage.

The team wants near-real-time detection of operational issues while keeping false positives manageable. The solution must use the correct CloudWatch, CloudTrail, Config, or tracing capability for the signal.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: AWS Budgets with budget alerts via SNS at threshold percentages
Cost Explorer alarms
CloudWatch billing alarm only (no granular alerts)
Trusted Advisor

questions.explanation: AWS Budgets: define cost/usage budget, set multiple alert thresholds (actual hoặc forecasted), notify via SNS/email. Granular alerts at 50%, 80%, 100%. CloudWatch billing alarm là 1 alarm đơn lẻ (us-east-1).

questions.question:
An organization wants to track AWS resource changes (e.g., who modified a security group, when) with detailed history.

The operations team needs actionable telemetry that can support alarms, dashboards, and incident investigations. The solution must avoid one-off log parsing scripts where managed AWS observability features are available.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: AWS Config with detailed configuration item history per resource
CloudTrail (API call history, but not resource configuration state)
VPC Flow Logs
GuardDuty

questions.explanation: AWS Config = configuration management database (CMDB): snapshot resource config theo time, history view, relationship graph. CloudTrail = API history (who called what), không track resource state. Cả hai bổ sung nhau.

questions.question:
A team uses CloudWatch alarms with SNS notifications. They want alert message to include the metric value at alarm time and dynamic context.

The company operates workloads in multiple accounts and needs consistent monitoring, logging, and audit visibility. The solution must preserve enough context to diagnose production incidents.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: CloudWatch alarm description supports static text; for dynamic content use SNS → Lambda subscriber that fetches metric/context and reformats
CloudWatch alarms support templated SNS messages out-of-the-box
EventBridge with input transformer is the cleaner approach for dynamic templating
Alarm reason field

questions.explanation: EventBridge integration với CloudWatch alarm state change: rule input transformer customize message format dùng path syntax. Cleaner than Lambda formatting. SNS direct subscriber nhận message JSON nguyên gốc của alarm.

questions.question:
A team needs to retain CloudWatch Logs for compliance (7 years) but most logs are rarely accessed after 30 days.

The team wants near-real-time detection of operational issues while keeping false positives manageable. The solution must use the correct CloudWatch, CloudTrail, Config, or tracing capability for the signal.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Set log group retention to 30 days; subscription filter exports older logs to S3 with Glacier Deep Archive lifecycle
Set retention to 7 years (expensive)
Delete logs after 30 days, lose history
Keep logs in CloudWatch indefinitely

questions.explanation: CloudWatch Logs đắt ($0.50/GB stored). S3 Glacier Deep Archive ~$0.00099/GB-month. Pattern: subscription filter → Kinesis Firehose → S3; log group retention ngắn (30 days hot search), S3 lifecycle Deep Archive cho long-term compliance.

questions.question:
An organization wants to manage on-call rotations, escalation paths, runbooks, and post-incident reviews natively in AWS.

The incident response process must be automated where safe, but the company must preserve evidence and avoid repeated manual actions. The solution should route events to the right remediation or notification workflow.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: AWS Systems Manager Incident Manager — response plans, escalation, runbooks, post-incident analysis
AWS Health Dashboard only
Trusted Advisor
EventBridge alone

questions.explanation: SSM Incident Manager: response plans (escalation contacts/teams), runbooks (SSM Automation), engagement engines (SMS, voice, email, chat), timeline + post-incident analysis. Native end-to-end incident lifecycle.

questions.question:
Operations teams need a single dashboard aggregating operational items (CloudWatch alarms, Config changes, EventBridge events) for triage.

The application uses event-driven components and asynchronous processing. The team needs a design that handles retries, failures, and replay without silently losing events.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Systems Manager OpsCenter aggregates OpsItems from multiple sources for centralized triage
CloudWatch Dashboards
AWS Health Dashboard
Personal Health Dashboard

questions.explanation: SSM OpsCenter: create OpsItems từ CloudWatch alarms, EventBridge events, Config rules, GuardDuty findings, Security Hub. Assign owner, runbook link, status. Single triage queue cho ops team.

questions.question:
A team wants Slack-based ChatOps with approvals (e.g., run an SSM runbook from chat with team approval).

The operations team wants to reduce mean time to recovery by using native AWS event sources and runbooks. The solution must be idempotent and safe to run during repeated events.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: AWS Chatbot — Slack/Microsoft Teams integration, supports CLI commands and approval workflows
Slack webhook with Lambda
Email-based approval
PagerDuty only

questions.explanation: AWS Chatbot connect Slack/MS Teams channel với AWS services. SNS/EventBridge notifications post tới channel. CLI in chat (aws ... commands), approval workflows. Reduces context switching.

questions.question:
A team uses SNS topics with multiple subscribers. They want different subscribers to receive different subsets of messages based on attributes (e.g., 'severity=critical' → on-call SMS; 'severity=warn' → email).

The incident response process must be automated where safe, but the company must preserve evidence and avoid repeated manual actions. The solution should route events to the right remediation or notification workflow.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: SNS Message Filtering Policy on each subscription (JSON policy matching message attributes)
Multiple SNS topics, one per severity
Lambda routing function
EventBridge rules only

questions.explanation: SNS Filter Policy (JSON) trên subscription: SNS chỉ deliver message nếu attributes match policy. Native fan-out filtering. Tránh tạo nhiều topic. Subscriber có thể subscribe cùng topic với filter khác nhau.

questions.question:
An application uses SQS FIFO queue for ordered message processing. Messages with the same MessageGroupId must process in order.

The application uses event-driven components and asynchronous processing. The team needs a design that handles retries, failures, and replay without silently losing events.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Within a MessageGroupId, processing is strictly ordered (single consumer at a time); different MessageGroupIds can process in parallel
All messages in queue are serial
FIFO has no concurrency control
Must use Standard queue

questions.explanation: SQS FIFO MessageGroupId định nghĩa ordering boundary. SQS deliver tin nhắn cùng MessageGroupId tuần tự cho 1 consumer. Group khác có thể parallel. Để max parallelism, dùng nhiều MessageGroupId (vd: userId).

questions.question:
A team wants to integrate a third-party SaaS (e.g., Datadog) sending events to AWS so that AWS resources react.

The operations team wants to reduce mean time to recovery by using native AWS event sources and runbooks. The solution must be idempotent and safe to run during repeated events.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: EventBridge Partner Event Source — third-party services publish to a partner event source, then route via EventBridge rules
Webhook to API Gateway only
Lambda function polling Datadog
SNS subscription

questions.explanation: EventBridge Partner Sources: 35+ SaaS partners (Datadog, Auth0, MongoDB, PagerDuty) publish events natively tới EventBridge. AWS account associate Partner Source, create rules. Cleaner than custom webhook.

questions.question:
A team needs to transform events from a source (e.g., DynamoDB Stream) and enrich them before sending to a target.

The incident response process must be automated where safe, but the company must preserve evidence and avoid repeated manual actions. The solution should route events to the right remediation or notification workflow.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: EventBridge Pipes — direct source-to-target with filter + enrich (Lambda/Step Functions/API destination) + transform
EventBridge rules only
Kinesis Data Streams
AWS Step Functions

questions.explanation: EventBridge Pipes: 1:1 integration source (DynamoDB Stream, Kinesis, SQS, MQ, Kafka) → optional filter → optional enrich (Lambda, SFN, API dest) → target. Replace boilerplate Lambda glue code. Native, fully managed.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A Step Functions workflow needs to retry a Task with exponential backoff up to 5 times, but limit max delay between retries to 60 seconds.

The application uses event-driven components and asynchronous processing. The team needs a design that handles retries, failures, and replay without silently losing events.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: IntervalSeconds (initial), MaxAttempts (5), BackoffRate (e.g., 2.0), MaxDelaySeconds (60)
Only MaxAttempts and IntervalSeconds
Use Wait state in a loop
Backoff is not configurable

questions.explanation: Retry field hỗ trợ: IntervalSeconds (1st retry delay), MaxAttempts, BackoffRate (multiplier), MaxDelaySeconds (cap), JitterStrategy. Full control. Tránh wait loop tay.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An EventBridge Scheduler runs a Lambda every hour. Sometimes Lambda fails silently. The team wants automatic retry + DLQ for failed invocations.

The operations team wants to reduce mean time to recovery by using native AWS event sources and runbooks. The solution must be idempotent and safe to run during repeated events.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Configure retry policy and Dead Letter Queue (SQS) on the schedule's target settings
EventBridge Scheduler doesn't support retries
Wrap Lambda in Step Functions
Use CloudWatch alarms

questions.explanation: EventBridge Scheduler target có retry policy (max retries, max event age) + DLQ (SQS) cho failed invocations. Native fault tolerance. Step Functions wrapper là overkill cho simple retry.

questions.question:
A team automates compliance: when AWS Config rule reports S3 bucket without encryption, automatically remediate by enabling default encryption.

The incident response process must be automated where safe, but the company must preserve evidence and avoid repeated manual actions. The solution should route events to the right remediation or notification workflow.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: AWS Config rule with associated Remediation Action pointing to SSM Automation document AWS-EnableS3BucketEncryption with auto-remediate enabled
Manual remediation only
EventBridge + Lambda from scratch
GuardDuty finding

questions.explanation: Pattern Config Remediation Action: gắn SSM Automation document tới rule, map params từ resource non-compliant, optional auto-remediate. AWS-managed automation docs có sẵn cho phổ biến case. Lambda from scratch không cần thiết.

questions.question:
A team runs a Lambda function as HTTP webhook receiver. They want to expose it without provisioning API Gateway.

The application uses event-driven components and asynchronous processing. The team needs a design that handles retries, failures, and replay without silently losing events.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Lambda Function URL — built-in HTTPS endpoint without API Gateway
EC2 reverse proxy
Lambda@Edge
Lambda doesn't expose HTTP without API Gateway

questions.explanation: Lambda Function URL: HTTPS endpoint native, không cần API Gateway. Auth: NONE hoặc AWS_IAM. Simpler/cheaper cho webhook đơn giản, không cần advanced API Gateway features (caching, throttling, transformation).

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An incident requires running an SSM Automation runbook ACROSS multiple AWS accounts (vd: snapshot every EC2 in Org).

The operations team wants to reduce mean time to recovery by using native AWS event sources and runbooks. The solution must be idempotent and safe to run during repeated events.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: SSM Automation execution with TargetLocations (cross-account/cross-region multi-execution)
Run separately in each account
Use Step Functions to loop through accounts
Not supported

questions.explanation: SSM Automation Multi-Account/Multi-Region: define TargetLocations (account list or Organization, region list). Execute runbook concurrently across accounts với centralized status. Requires execution role trong target accounts.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An EventBridge rule must filter events where 'detail.amount' is between 100 and 1000.

The incident response process must be automated where safe, but the company must preserve evidence and avoid repeated manual actions. The solution should route events to the right remediation or notification workflow.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Regex matching
Use jq in Lambda
Custom Lambda filter

questions.explanation: EventBridge content-based filtering hỗ trợ numeric matcher: numeric với operator (>, >=, <, <=) và values. Cũng có anything-but, exists, prefix, suffix, equals-ignore-case. Lambda filter là overkill.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. Step Functions Standard Workflow processes long-running orders (24 hours, many steps). Express Workflow runs short-lived high-volume webhooks.

The application uses event-driven components and asynchronous processing. The team needs a design that handles retries, failures, and replay without silently losing events.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Standard: durable, up to 1 year execution; Express: up to 5 minutes, higher throughput, lower cost per execution, logged to CloudWatch Logs
Both have identical features
Express is more expensive
Standard is for testing only

questions.explanation: Standard: long-running (≤1 năm), exactly-once, durable state. Express: ≤5 phút, at-least-once async / sync, optimized cho high-volume / low-latency (vd: streaming, mobile backend), không lưu execution detail (chỉ logs).

questions.question:
A team wants to send notifications when an Amazon S3 bucket policy changes.

The operations team wants to reduce mean time to recovery by using native AWS event sources and runbooks. The solution must be idempotent and safe to run during repeated events.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: CloudTrail emits PutBucketPolicy event → EventBridge rule → SNS topic
S3 Event Notifications (only for object events, not bucket-level policy)
Config snapshot daily
Athena query weekly

questions.explanation: S3 Event Notifications cho object events (created, deleted, etc.), không bucket-level config. Bucket policy change ghi nhận trong CloudTrail (PutBucketPolicy). EventBridge match event → notify. Near-realtime (~giây).

questions.question:
A team uses Step Functions with a Map state to process 1000 items in parallel. They want to limit concurrency to 50 simultaneous Lambda invocations to avoid throttling downstream.

The incident response process must be automated where safe, but the company must preserve evidence and avoid repeated manual actions. The solution should route events to the right remediation or notification workflow.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Set MaxConcurrency: 50 on the Map state
Set Lambda reserved concurrency to 50 (also valid, but more permanent)
Use a Wait state between items
Map state has no concurrency control

questions.explanation: Map state MaxConcurrency field giới hạn số iteration parallel. 0 = no limit (default inline Map = 40 max). Distributed Map có MaxConcurrency cao hơn. Lambda reserved concurrency là permanent limit, ảnh hưởng mọi caller, kém linh hoạt.

questions.question:
A team uses Lambda for high-volume event processing. Some events cause errors that should retry up to 3 times, then move to DLQ for manual review. The Lambda is invoked asynchronously by EventBridge. Where is retry/DLQ configured.

The application uses event-driven components and asynchronous processing. The team needs a design that handles retries, failures, and replay without silently losing events.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Lambda function event invoke config: MaximumRetryAttempts (0-2, default 2) + on-failure destination (SQS as DLQ) for async invocations
EventBridge has no retry config
Add try/catch in code
Use Lambda function URL

questions.explanation: Async Lambda invocation config: MaxRetryAttempts (default 2, max 2 → tổng 3 lần thử), MaxEventAge, OnFailure (SQS/SNS/Lambda/EventBridge destination). EventBridge target retry policy là separate layer. Combine cho fine control.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An incident response procedure requires isolating a compromised EC2 instance from network instantly.

The operations team wants to reduce mean time to recovery by using native AWS event sources and runbooks. The solution must be idempotent and safe to run during repeated events.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: SSM Automation runbook: detach IAM instance profile + replace security groups with a 'quarantine' SG (no egress/ingress) + create EBS snapshot for forensics
Stop the instance (loses ephemeral evidence)
Disable VPC
Delete the instance

questions.explanation: Quarantine pattern: replace SG (network isolated), detach IAM profile (prevent further AWS API misuse), snapshot EBS (forensic evidence). EC2 vẫn running để giữ memory/processes nếu cần memory dump. AWS có sample automation docs cho việc này.

questions.question:
A team uses AWS Step Functions to orchestrate an ETL pipeline. One step calls a third-party API that has a 429 (Too Many Requests) rate limit.

The incident response process must be automated where safe, but the company must preserve evidence and avoid repeated manual actions. The solution should route events to the right remediation or notification workflow.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Retry { ErrorEquals: ['States.TaskFailed'] with caught error matching '429', IntervalSeconds: 2, BackoffRate: 2.0, MaxAttempts: 5 }
Disable retries
Manual retry only
Run as Express workflow

questions.explanation: Retry với BackoffRate 2 (exponential): 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 32s. ErrorEquals match specific error (vd: custom error name). MaxAttempts 5 đủ để vượt qua rate limit window. Pattern dù third-party API throttle.

questions.question:
A team wants to centralize alerting from multiple AWS sources (CloudWatch alarms, GuardDuty findings, Health events) to a single Slack channel with deduplication. Architecture.

The application uses event-driven components and asynchronous processing. The team needs a design that handles retries, failures, and replay without silently losing events.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Each source → EventBridge (or SNS) → single Lambda fan-in → AWS Chatbot Slack integration with rate-limiting/dedup logic in Lambda
Subscribe Slack to every SNS topic directly
PagerDuty integration
Email Slack

questions.explanation: Fan-in pattern: nguồn → 1 hub Lambda/Chatbot. Lambda có thể dedupe (DynamoDB TTL cache), format, route. AWS Chatbot là last-mile to Slack. Direct SNS Slack tạo noise và không có dedup.

questions.question:
A team needs scheduled tasks (cron-like) that survive AWS region failure (vd: must run trong us-west-2 nếu us-east-1 down).

The operations team wants to reduce mean time to recovery by using native AWS event sources and runbooks. The solution must be idempotent and safe to run during repeated events.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Deploy EventBridge Scheduler in multiple regions; coordinate via DynamoDB Global Table to ensure single execution
Single region scheduler with backup region
Cron on EC2 (SPOF)
AWS Backup

questions.explanation: EventBridge Scheduler regional service. Multi-region cron HA pattern: scheduler trong N region, target Lambda check distributed lock trong DynamoDB Global Table (conditional write trên 'last_run_timestamp') để ensure single execution. Hoặc accept idempotent target.

questions.question:
A team wants to gracefully drain ECS Fargate tasks during deployment to ensure in-flight requests complete.

The incident response process must be automated where safe, but the company must preserve evidence and avoid repeated manual actions. The solution should route events to the right remediation or notification workflow.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: ALB target group deregistration delay + ECS service stopTimeout container property + signal handlers in app (SIGTERM)
Stop tasks immediately
Disable health check
Use Lambda

questions.explanation: Graceful drain: ALB deregistration delay (target stops accepting new conn, drain existing). ECS gửi SIGTERM tới container, đợi stopTimeout (default 30s) rồi SIGKILL. App phải catch SIGTERM, complete in-flight requests, close. Pattern zero-downtime deploy.

questions.question:
A team wants to identify IAM roles in their account that have access to S3 buckets in EXTERNAL accounts (cross-account exposure).

The organization has compliance requirements for encryption, access control, and continuous detection. The DevOps engineer must choose managed AWS security services where possible.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: IAM Access Analyzer — external access findings for resource policies (S3, IAM, KMS, Lambda, SQS, Secrets Manager)
AWS Config rules
GuardDuty
Manual audit

questions.explanation: IAM Access Analyzer (free): scan resource-based policies trong account/Organization, identify access từ external principals (account, public). Generate findings actionable. Hỗ trợ 8+ resource types. Cũng có policy validation + unused access analyzer.

questions.question:
A team uses IAM Identity Center to assign permissions across the Organization. They want HR's permissions to vary by department (department=Engineering vs Finance), encoded in the identity provider.

The environment includes multiple workload accounts. The solution must enforce guardrails consistently while preserving evidence for investigations and audit reviews.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) in IAM Identity Center: pass attributes from IdP as session tags, reference in permission set policy
Create one permission set per department (many)
Use SCPs
Federate per department

questions.explanation: ABAC: Identity Center pass IdP attributes (vd: department, costCenter) as session tags. Permission set policy reference aws:PrincipalTag/department, allow theo tag. Scale: 1 permission set cho mọi department, đỡ duplicate.

questions.question:
An organization wants to delegate AWS Config administration from the management account to a security-focused member account.

The security team requires least privilege, centralized visibility, and auditable controls across accounts. The solution must not rely on shared long-term credentials or public access.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Register a delegated administrator for AWS Config service in AWS Organizations
Share management account credentials
Cannot be delegated
Use IAM roles only

questions.explanation: AWS Organizations cho phép register Delegated Administrator cho nhiều services (Config, Security Hub, GuardDuty, Macie, IAM Access Analyzer, etc.). Member account đó có quyền admin cho service đó cross-org. Reduce reliance trên management account.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A KMS customer-managed key (CMK) is used across us-east-1 and eu-west-1 for the same encrypted data. Manual cross-region key replication is operational burden.

The organization has compliance requirements for encryption, access control, and continuous detection. The DevOps engineer must choose managed AWS security services where possible.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: KMS multi-Region keys (MRKs) — same key material across regions, same key ID, independent policies; encrypt in one, decrypt in another
Single-region keys with manual replication
KMS doesn't support multi-region
Use SSM Parameter Store

questions.explanation: KMS Multi-Region Keys: replica keys ở regions khác share key material, có cùng Key ID (with mrk- prefix). Encrypt at region A → decrypt at region B without re-encrypt. Phù hợp cho global DR + S3 CRR với SSE-KMS.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A KMS CMK has both a key policy and an attached IAM policy on the role. The key policy denies all access except to root account. The IAM policy allows kms:Decrypt. Can the role decrypt.

The environment includes multiple workload accounts. The solution must enforce guardrails consistently while preserving evidence for investigations and audit reviews.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: No — key policy is authoritative; if key policy doesn't grant access to the role (or to IAM principals in general), IAM policy alone cannot grant access
Yes — IAM policy wins
Yes — root account allows all
Depends on the region

questions.explanation: KMS key policy MUST allow access; IAM policy alone không đủ. Pattern phổ biến: key policy allow root + 'kms:CreateGrant' để IAM policies có thể thêm permission. Nếu key policy strict, role không decrypt được.

questions.question:
A team uses Secrets Manager for an Oracle database password (not RDS). They want automatic rotation every 90 days.

The security team requires least privilege, centralized visibility, and auditable controls across accounts. The solution must not rely on shared long-term credentials or public access.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Custom Lambda rotation function implementing the rotation steps (createSecret, setSecret, testSecret, finishSecret) configured with the secret
RDS automated rotation handles all DBs
Manual rotation only
Disable rotation

questions.explanation: Secrets Manager native rotation cho RDS/Aurora/DocumentDB/Redshift. Cho Oracle on EC2 hoặc non-AWS DB, viết Lambda rotation function với 4 step pattern: createSecret (new version), setSecret (apply to DB), testSecret, finishSecret (move AWSCURRENT label).

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. GuardDuty discovers a Trojan in an EC2 instance via EBS volume scanning.

The organization has compliance requirements for encryption, access control, and continuous detection. The DevOps engineer must choose managed AWS security services where possible.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: GuardDuty Malware Protection — scans EBS volumes attached to EC2 / Lambda / containers on findings or on-demand
Amazon Inspector
Macie
AWS Shield

questions.explanation: GuardDuty Malware Protection scan EBS volume (sao chép snapshot + scan signature). Trigger từ GuardDuty finding hoặc on-demand. Native, không cần install agent. Đáng kể nâng visibility threats.

questions.question:
A team wants continuous scanning of all EC2 instances, ECR repos, and Lambda functions for software vulnerabilities.

The environment includes multiple workload accounts. The solution must enforce guardrails consistently while preserving evidence for investigations and audit reviews.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Amazon Inspector v2 (continuous scanning for EC2, ECR, Lambda)
Inspector v1 (legacy, EC2 only)
GuardDuty
Macie

questions.explanation: Inspector v2 continuous, agentless cho ECR, SSM-agent based cho EC2, snapshot scan cho Lambda. CVE database + AWS findings. Centralized findings in Security Hub. V1 deprecated.

questions.question:
A team runs Macie discovery jobs and notices cost is high because it re-scans the same buckets repeatedly.

The security team requires least privilege, centralized visibility, and auditable controls across accounts. The solution must not rely on shared long-term credentials or public access.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Use one-time discovery jobs for snapshots + enable automated sensitive data discovery for ongoing coverage (samples objects intelligently, lower cost)
Disable Macie
Scan more frequently for cost reduction
Manual scanning

questions.explanation: Macie automated sensitive data discovery (newer feature) sample objects intelligently, cover toàn bộ account/Org với cost predictable thấp hơn full one-time job. Combine: one-time deep scan + automated daily.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A compliance team requires PCI DSS controls visible.

The organization has compliance requirements for encryption, access control, and continuous detection. The DevOps engineer must choose managed AWS security services where possible.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: AWS Security Hub with PCI DSS standard enabled — maps Config rules and other controls to PCI requirements
Trusted Advisor
Macie
Manual spreadsheet

questions.explanation: Security Hub có pre-built standards: AWS Foundational Security Best Practices, CIS AWS Benchmarks, PCI DSS, NIST. Mỗi standard map control → automated checks. Compliance dashboard, score, failed controls. Native compliance reporting.

questions.question:
An organization wants central management of WAF rules across 100 accounts, automatically applying a baseline WAF web ACL to every CloudFront and ALB.

The environment includes multiple workload accounts. The solution must enforce guardrails consistently while preserving evidence for investigations and audit reviews.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: AWS Firewall Manager — policies for WAF, Shield Advanced, security groups, Network Firewall across Organization
WAF console manually
CloudFormation StackSets only
Lambda automation

questions.explanation: Firewall Manager (FMS): central manage WAF/Shield/SG/Network Firewall/DNS Firewall qua Organizations. Define policy ở Firewall Manager admin account, auto-apply tới resources matching tag/account. Continuous enforcement.

questions.question:
An application needs to filter network traffic at VPC level with stateful inspection (e.g., deep packet inspection, intrusion prevention).

The security team requires least privilege, centralized visibility, and auditable controls across accounts. The solution must not rely on shared long-term credentials or public access.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: AWS Network Firewall (stateful/stateless inspection at VPC level with Suricata-compatible rules)
Security Group (stateful but limited to L4)
Network ACL (stateless)
WAF (L7 HTTP only)

questions.explanation: AWS Network Firewall managed VPC-level firewall: stateful rules (Suricata format), stateless rules, IDS/IPS, deep packet inspection. SG/NACL hoạt động L3/L4 đơn giản. WAF L7 HTTP, không phải L3.

questions.question:
A team has an API Gateway endpoint exposed publicly. They want to throttle abusive clients dynamically (vd: >1000 req/5 min = block).

The organization has compliance requirements for encryption, access control, and continuous detection. The DevOps engineer must choose managed AWS security services where possible.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: AWS WAF rate-based rule (count requests by IP within 5-minute window, block when threshold exceeded)
API Gateway usage plan (per API key, not per IP)
Lambda authorizer custom logic
CloudFront throttling

questions.explanation: WAF rate-based rule: tự track request rate per IP (5-min window), block when exceeds threshold. Dynamic, automated. API Gateway usage plan dùng API key, không applicable cho anon abuse. Lambda authorizer cho mỗi request là tốn kém.

questions.question:
A team wants to use AWS-managed WAF rules to protect against common threats (SQL injection, XSS, bad bots) without manually writing rules.

The environment includes multiple workload accounts. The solution must enforce guardrails consistently while preserving evidence for investigations and audit reviews.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: AWS Managed Rule Groups (e.g., AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet, AWSManagedRulesSQLiRuleSet, AWSManagedRulesBotControlRuleSet)
Custom rules only
GuardDuty
Inspector

questions.explanation: WAF Managed Rule Groups: maintained bởi AWS (free hoặc paid). Common, Known Bad Inputs, SQLi, Anonymous IP list, Bot Control, Account Takeover Prevention, Fraud Control. One-click add. AWS update threat signatures.

questions.question:
A team needs to perform a compliance audit, requiring evidence-of-control collection (vd: 'show all KMS key rotation enabled').

The security team requires least privilege, centralized visibility, and auditable controls across accounts. The solution must not rely on shared long-term credentials or public access.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: AWS Audit Manager — assessment frameworks (PCI, GDPR, HIPAA, SOC, custom) automatically collect evidence from Config/CloudTrail/Security Hub
AWS Artifact (compliance reports for AWS, not customer workloads)
Manually export reports
Trusted Advisor

questions.explanation: AWS Audit Manager: pre-built frameworks + custom. Automatically map controls → AWS API calls / Config / CloudTrail. Continuous evidence collection. Assessment report exportable cho auditor. Artifact là cho AWS's own compliance docs.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An auditor requires AWS SOC 2 Type II report. Where to download.

The organization has compliance requirements for encryption, access control, and continuous detection. The DevOps engineer must choose managed AWS security services where possible.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: AWS Artifact (self-service portal for AWS compliance reports: SOC, ISO, PCI, FedRAMP, etc.)
Audit Manager
Email AWS Support
AWS Marketplace

questions.explanation: AWS Artifact: self-service downloads of AWS Compliance reports (SOC 1/2/3, ISO 27001/27017/27018, PCI DSS, FedRAMP, HIPAA, etc.). Cần NDA acceptance trong console. Khác Audit Manager (customer's own compliance audit).

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A VPC endpoint (Gateway type for S3) is used to keep traffic private. The team wants to restrict that only specific S3 buckets can be accessed via this endpoint.

The environment includes multiple workload accounts. The solution must enforce guardrails consistently while preserving evidence for investigations and audit reviews.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: VPC endpoint policy on the gateway endpoint (resource-based JSON policy with Allow/Deny for specific buckets)
Security group on endpoint (gateway endpoints don't use SGs)
IAM policy on user only
Bucket policy only

questions.explanation: VPC endpoint policy IAM JSON: control resource (bucket/prefix) cho phép qua endpoint. Defense-in-depth combined with bucket policy. Gateway endpoint không có SG (use endpoint policy). Interface endpoint có SG + endpoint policy.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A vendor needs to assume a role in your AWS account to manage resources on your behalf. To prevent confused deputy attacks (vendor's other clients accidentally accessing your account),.

The security team requires least privilege, centralized visibility, and auditable controls across accounts. The solution must not rely on shared long-term credentials or public access.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Condition with sts:ExternalId requiring a unique secret you and the vendor share
MFA only
Allow root principal
Use access keys instead

questions.explanation: External ID: unique secret bạn cung cấp cho vendor. Vendor MUST pass ExternalId trong AssumeRole call. Mitigate confused deputy: vendor's other clients không thể assume role nếu không biết ExternalId. AWS recommended cho third-party access.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An IAM role's exposed credentials were detected (via GitHub scanning). AWS automatically applied a quarantine policy.

The organization has compliance requirements for encryption, access control, and continuous detection. The DevOps engineer must choose managed AWS security services where possible.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: AWSCompromisedKeyQuarantineV2 (or similar AWS-managed policy that denies most powerful actions while allowing the user to rotate keys)
AdministratorAccess
Deny all
ReadOnlyAccess

questions.explanation: AWS detect exposed key (qua scanning hợp tác), attach AWSCompromisedKeyQuarantineV2 (deny high-risk actions like create IAM, create EC2 large, etc., nhưng allow rotate key, list, basic read). Email notify + AWS Health event. User PHẢI rotate key + remove policy.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An Organizations SCP attached to an OU denies s3:DeleteBucket. A member account's user has IAM policy allowing s3:* on all resources. Can the user delete a bucket.

The environment includes multiple workload accounts. The solution must enforce guardrails consistently while preserving evidence for investigations and audit reviews.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: No — SCP is the maximum permissions ceiling; even Allow in IAM cannot grant what SCP denies
Yes — IAM is final authority
Yes if user is admin
Depends on resource policy

questions.explanation: SCP định permission ceiling cho account. IAM policy bên trong cho phép quyền nằm TRONG ceiling đó. SCP Deny luôn thắng. Root user trong account cũng không bypass SCP. Đây là cốt lõi của centralized governance.

questions.question:
A team uses CloudHSM for FIPS 140-2 Level 3 hardware security module. They want CloudFormation to provision an HSM cluster automatically.

The security team requires least privilege, centralized visibility, and auditable controls across accounts. The solution must not rely on shared long-term credentials or public access.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: AWS::CloudHSM::Cluster and AWS::CloudHSM::Hsm in CloudFormation
AWS::KMS::Key (not HSM)
HSM cannot be provisioned with CFN
Use Terraform

questions.explanation: CloudFormation hỗ trợ AWS::CloudHSMv2::Cluster + AWS::CloudHSMv2::Hsm. Tự động hóa provision cluster + HSM ở subnets. Post-create cần initialize (CO setup, crypto user) qua AWS CLI / CloudHSM client.

questions.question:
A team needs to identify which IAM roles in their account have NOT been used in the last 90 days (to revoke).

The organization has compliance requirements for encryption, access control, and continuous detection. The DevOps engineer must choose managed AWS security services where possible.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: IAM Access Analyzer — Unused Access feature finds inactive users/roles/permissions
CloudTrail manual analysis
AWS Trusted Advisor
GuardDuty

questions.explanation: IAM Access Analyzer 'Unused Access analyzer': identify inactive IAM users, roles, access keys, permissions (Lambda, S3). Recommendations để clean up. Pay-per-analyzer cost. Native discovery without manual CloudTrail dig.

questions.question:
A team wants to enforce MFA for IAM users when calling sensitive APIs (vd: deleting S3 bucket).

The environment includes multiple workload accounts. The solution must enforce guardrails consistently while preserving evidence for investigations and audit reviews.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent in IAM policy condition (allow action only when true)
aws:SourceIp
aws:SecureTransport
aws:Username

questions.explanation: IAM policy condition aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent (boolean) — true khi caller authenticated với MFA. Pattern: deny sensitive action if not MFA: { "Condition": { "BoolIfExists": { "aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent": "false" } } }.

questions.question:
A team uses GuardDuty and wants to automatically respond to a finding 'CryptoCurrency:EC2/BitcoinTool.B!DNS' (instance contacting crypto-mining DNS).

The security team requires least privilege, centralized visibility, and auditable controls across accounts. The solution must not rely on shared long-term credentials or public access.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: EventBridge rule on GuardDuty finding → SSM Automation runbook to quarantine instance (replace SG, detach IAM, snapshot EBS) + create OpsCenter ticket
Manual investigation only
Disable GuardDuty
Whitelist the DNS

questions.explanation: Pattern automated incident response: GuardDuty → EventBridge → SSM Automation quarantine + SNS notify + OpsCenter ops item. AWS có sample runbooks (AWSConfigRemediation-*, AWS-StopEC2Instance). Quan trọng: snapshot trước khi quarantine để forensics.

questions.question:
A DevOps team manages a CodePipeline pipeline that deploys a microservice to three AWS accounts: dev, staging, and prod. Each deployment uses CloudFormation. The team wants the prod deployment to require human approval, and they want approvers to be notified via Slack with a link to the CloudFormation change set summary.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Add a CloudFormation 'Create Change Set' action followed by a Manual Approval action in the prod stage. Configure the Manual Approval action to publish to an SNS topic subscribed to AWS Chatbot, which posts to Slack.
Add a Lambda invoke action that creates a change set and posts a Slack message via webhook. Use a second Lambda to poll for approval before executing the change set.
Configure CodePipeline to send EventBridge events for stage state changes to a Lambda function that calls the Slack API and waits for a reply before continuing.
Replace CodePipeline with Step Functions. Use a Wait-for-Callback task with Slack interaction.

questions.explanation: CodePipeline có sẵn 2 action chuẩn cho yêu cầu này: 'CloudFormation Create Change Set' (deploymentMode = CHANGE_SET_REPLACE) và 'Manual Approval'. Manual Approval action có field 'NotificationArn' để publish lên SNS, và AWS Chatbot subscribe SNS rồi post sang Slack — zero code. B/C/D đều phải tự viết Lambda/webhook handling, tốn code hơn nhiều.

questions.question:
A company uses CodePipeline in account A to build artifacts and deploy them to accounts B and C via CloudFormation. The cross-account deployment is failing with 'Access Denied' when CodePipeline tries to upload artifacts to the S3 bucket.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which combination of steps should a DevOps engineer take to meet these requirements? (Choose THREE.)

options.text: The artifact S3 bucket in account A must have a bucket policy granting s3:GetObject and s3:PutObject to the IAM roles in accounts B and C.
The artifacts must be encrypted with a customer-managed KMS key whose key policy allows access from accounts B and C.
The CodePipeline service role in account A must have permission to assume a role in accounts B and C.
Accounts B and C must be members of the same AWS Organization as account A.
The artifact S3 bucket must be replicated to accounts B and C using S3 Cross-Region Replication.
Public access must be enabled on the artifact S3 bucket.

questions.explanation: Cross-account artifact 3 yêu cầu cốt lõi: (A) S3 bucket policy cho phép cross-account read/write, (B) artifacts BẮT BUỘC dùng CMK KMS (không dùng được SSE-S3) với key policy cross-account, (C) CodePipeline role assume role cross-account. D sai vì không cần Organization; E sai vì không cần replication; F là anti-pattern bảo mật.

questions.question:
A team's CodeBuild project takes 12 minutes to run because it downloads ~800 MB of npm dependencies on every build. Source code is small (~5 MB). The team wants to cut build time without changing the application code.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Enable CodeBuild local caching with the 'LOCAL_CUSTOM_CACHE' mode and specify node_modules in the buildspec cache paths.
Switch to a larger compute type (BUILD_GENERAL1_LARGE) to download dependencies in parallel.
Use CodeBuild source caching to cache the Git source in S3.
Disable dependency installation and ship dependencies as a Lambda layer instead.

questions.explanation: CodeBuild local cache (LOCAL_CUSTOM_CACHE) cache node_modules giữa các build trên cùng host → bỏ qua npm install nếu lock file không đổi. Cấu hình chỉ là khai báo `cache.paths` trong buildspec.yml. B (compute type lớn hơn) không giải quyết network bottleneck. C cache SOURCE không phải dependencies. D không phù hợp ứng dụng non-Lambda.

questions.question:
A DevOps engineer needs CodeBuild to access an RDS MySQL database in a private VPC subnet during integration tests. The build also needs to pull base images from Docker Hub.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Put CodeBuild in the same private subnet as RDS. Add a NAT Gateway in a public subnet for Docker Hub access. Grant the CodeBuild service role ec2:CreateNetworkInterface, ec2:DescribeDhcpOptions, ec2:DescribeNetworkInterfaces, ec2:DeleteNetworkInterface, ec2:DescribeSubnets, ec2:DescribeSecurityGroups, ec2:DescribeVpcs.
Put CodeBuild in a public subnet with an Elastic IP for Docker Hub. RDS security group allows inbound from the CodeBuild ENI.
Use VPC peering between CodeBuild's managed VPC and the RDS VPC.
Use a Transit Gateway with a Docker Hub VPC endpoint.

questions.explanation: CodeBuild VPC mode chạy ENI trong private subnet bạn chỉ định. Để truy cập internet (Docker Hub) phải có NAT Gateway ở public subnet và route 0.0.0.0/0 đi qua NAT. Service role cần các ec2:* permissions để tạo/xóa ENI. B sai vì CodeBuild không support public subnet với public IP. C/D không tồn tại (CodeBuild không có managed VPC, không có Docker Hub VPC endpoint).

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A buildspec.yml references a database password stored in AWS Secrets Manager as a secret with name 'prod/db' and JSON key 'password'. The team wants the password to NOT appear in CloudWatch Logs build output.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text:
env:
  secrets-manager:
    DB_PASS: prod/db:password

env:
  variables:
    DB_PASS: $(aws secretsmanager get-secret-value --secret-id prod/db --query SecretString)

env:
  parameter-store:
    DB_PASS: prod/db

env:
  exported-variables:
    - DB_PASS

questions.explanation: Buildspec hỗ trợ `env.secrets-manager` để inject trực tiếp từ Secrets Manager với syntax `<env_name>: <secret_id>:<json_key>`. Giá trị inject sẽ TỰ ĐỘNG bị mask trong CloudWatch Logs. B là plain variables → log không mask. C là Parameter Store (sai service). D dùng cho export biến giữa các phase.

questions.question:
A company deploys a stateless web app to an EC2 Auto Scaling Group fronted by an ALB. They want zero-downtime deployments with the ability to automatically roll back if HTTP 5xx error rate exceeds 1% during deployment.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which combination of steps should a DevOps engineer take to meet these requirements? (Choose TWO.)

options.text: Use the Blue/Green deployment type with CodeDeployDefault.HalfAtATime and automatic rollback on alarm.
Use the Blue/Green deployment type with manual termination of the original instances after 30 minutes.
Configure CloudWatch alarms on ALB HTTPCode_Target_5XX_Count. Enable 'Rollback when a deployment fails' and 'Rollback when alarm thresholds are met' on the deployment group.
Use In-Place deployment with CodeDeployDefault.AllAtOnce.
Use In-Place deployment with CodeDeployDefault.OneAtATime and set a lifecycle hook to validate.

questions.explanation: Yêu cầu zero-downtime → cần Blue/Green (A) vì in-place luôn giảm capacity tạm thời. Automatic rollback dựa trên error rate → cần CloudWatch alarm trên 5xx và enable rollback-on-alarm (C). B sai vì manual termination không phải zero-downtime control flow. D AllAtOnce gây downtime. E in-place vẫn có downtime nhỏ, và không match yêu cầu zero-downtime.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A Lambda function is deployed with CodeDeploy using the 'Canary10Percent5Minutes' configuration. After 5 minutes, the canary still has 10% traffic.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: The BeforeAllowTraffic hook failed validation, pausing traffic shift.
The Canary configuration requires manual approval to proceed to 100%.
An automatic rollback was triggered by a CloudWatch alarm associated with the deployment group, shifting traffic back to the previous version.
Lambda concurrency limit was reached, preventing further traffic shift.

questions.explanation: Canary10Percent5Minutes: 10% trong 5 phút, sau đó 100%. Nếu sau 5 phút vẫn 10%, deployment KHÔNG còn ở 10% — đã rollback hoặc fail. Đáp án đúng nhất là rollback do alarm: traffic về 0% trên version mới (alias trỏ lại version cũ). A sai vì BeforeAllowTraffic chạy TRƯỚC khi shift traffic. B sai vì Canary không cần manual approval. D không phải pattern điển hình.

questions.question:
A DevOps engineer is configuring CodeDeploy for an ECS service running on Fargate behind an ALB. The appspec.yml needs to reference.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: TaskDefinition (with ContainerName, ContainerPort, and PlatformVersion).
EC2TagSet specifying the cluster tag.
AutoScalingGroup with the Fargate capacity provider.
LambdaAlias with traffic shifting config.

questions.explanation: Appspec cho ECS deployment có Resources: TaskDefinition (ARN của task def mới) + LoadBalancerInfo (ContainerName, ContainerPort) + PlatformVersion (cho Fargate). B chỉ áp dụng EC2/On-prem in-place. C không hợp lý vì Fargate không dùng ASG. D dành cho Lambda deployment.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. After a CodeDeploy in-place deployment to a 10-instance ASG fails on instance 6 (AllAtOnce), the deployment status shows 'Failed'. The application is now in a mixed state.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: It creates a NEW deployment that re-installs the PREVIOUS application revision to all instances in the deployment group.
It uses snapshots to restore EBS volumes to pre-deployment state.
It terminates the failed instances and lets the ASG replace them.
It marks the deployment as 'Rolled back' but leaves instances in the mixed state for manual remediation.

questions.explanation: CodeDeploy rollback KHÔNG phải undo — nó tạo deployment MỚI deploy lại revision trước đó. Tốn time + appspec hooks phải idempotent. B không phải behavior CodeDeploy. C là ASG self-healing chứ không phải rollback. D mâu thuẫn với option đã enable.

questions.question:
A company runs multiple microservices on ECS. They want to standardize CI/CD across all services using CodePipeline. Common requirements: artifacts in central account, deployments fan out to multiple target accounts, audit log of every deployment, and rollback button.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which combination of steps should a DevOps engineer take to meet these requirements? (Choose TWO.)

options.text: Use a single CodePipeline per service in the central account with cross-account CodeDeploy actions to target accounts. Enable EventBridge to capture pipeline state changes to a central CloudWatch Log group.
Use CodeStar Connections with GitHub as source, CodeBuild for image build, and Amazon ECR replication for cross-account image distribution.
Run Jenkins on EC2 in each target account; sync pipelines via Git.
Use AWS Proton with self-managed templates for every service.
Use AWS Service Catalog products for each service and let users self-deploy.

questions.explanation: A: CodePipeline ở central account với cross-account CodeDeploy + EventBridge audit là pattern AWS recommend, ít overhead. B: CodeStar Connections (GitHub) + ECR replication tự động distribute image cross-account → không phải push lặp lại. C: Jenkins per account = nhiều overhead. D: Proton phù hợp template-driven app nhưng heavy cho microservices đơn giản. E: Service Catalog dành cho infra products không phải CI/CD.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A CodePipeline pipeline uses a CloudFormation deploy action with 'CREATE_UPDATE' deployment mode. The stack update fails halfway through and is left in UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED state. The pipeline subsequent runs all fail at the deploy action.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Use the CloudFormation 'continue-update-rollback' action with --resources-to-skip pointing to the failed resource, then re-trigger the pipeline.
Delete the stack and recreate it via the pipeline's CHANGE_SET_REPLACE mode.
Manually edit the stack template in the AWS Console to remove the failed resource.
Increase the CodePipeline action timeout.

questions.explanation: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED là deadlock state. Cách thoát chuẩn: `continue-update-rollback --resources-to-skip` để skip resource bị treo → stack về UPDATE_ROLLBACK_COMPLETE, sẵn sàng cho update mới. B (delete+recreate) gây mất resources, là last resort. C không thể edit stack qua console khi state này. D không liên quan.

questions.question:
A team wants to trigger a CodePipeline run automatically whenever a Git tag matching the pattern 'release-*' is pushed to their CodeCommit repo. They do NOT want runs triggered on regular commits to any branch.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Create an EventBridge rule matching CodeCommit 'referenceCreated' events where 'referenceType' is 'tag' and 'referenceName' starts with 'release-'. Target: StartPipelineExecution on the pipeline.
Enable the default CodeCommit source action polling and add a Lambda filter that aborts the pipeline if the branch is not a tag.
Add a CodeBuild pre-step that runs 'git describe' and exits early unless a release tag is present.
Configure a CloudWatch Logs metric filter on CodeCommit API logs for 'CreateBranch' events.

questions.explanation: EventBridge với detail-type 'CodeCommit Repository State Change' filter trên referenceType=tag và referenceName pattern là cách native, không cần code. B tốn run lãng phí. C tốn build minute và không reliable. D nhầm CreateBranch và không lọc được tag.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A buildspec.yml uses `phases.install`, `phases.pre_build`, `phases.build`, and `phases.post_build`. The 'build' phase fails.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Skips remaining build commands but STILL runs 'post_build' phase. Marks the overall build as FAILED.
Stops immediately and skips 'post_build'. Marks the build FAILED.
Retries 'build' phase up to 3 times before failing.
Continues to 'post_build' and marks the build SUCCEEDED if post_build passes.

questions.explanation: CodeBuild luôn chạy post_build dù build phase fail — để bạn có thể upload reports, gửi notification, cleanup. Tuy nhiên overall status = FAILED. Lưu ý: `$CODEBUILD_BUILD_SUCCEEDING` env var = 0 trong post_build nếu build failed, dùng để branch logic. B/C/D đều sai về behavior thực tế.

questions.question:
A company uses CodeArtifact for npm packages. Their CodeBuild builds run `npm install` against an internal CodeArtifact repository that has an upstream of the public npm registry. A new build fails with 'No package found' for a package that exists publicly. Most likely cause.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: The package was never requested before, so CodeArtifact's lazy upstream fetch hasn't ingested it. The user must run a one-time fetch (e.g., via `aws codeartifact get-package-version-asset`) or grant the build role permission to read external connections.
CodeArtifact does not support npm packages.
The CodeBuild env is missing AWS_REGION environment variable.
CodeArtifact requires the package to be republished as a CodeArtifact-native package, not fetched from upstream.

questions.explanation: CodeArtifact upstream ingest LAZY — package công cộng chỉ được pull khi consumer yêu cầu và build role có permission `codeartifact:ReadFromRepository` + external connection access. Nếu role thiếu quyền 'access external repository' thì sẽ ra lỗi 'no package found'. B sai (npm fully supported). D sai về cơ chế upstream.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An Elastic Beanstalk environment running a Node.js app needs zero-downtime deployments AND the ability to test the new version in production with real traffic before fully cutting over.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Traffic Splitting (canary)
All-at-once
Rolling
Rolling with additional batch

questions.explanation: Traffic Splitting trong EB tạo ASG mới với version mới, route % traffic configurable trong khoảng thời gian — đúng nghĩa canary. All-at-once gây downtime. Rolling thay thế tuần tự nhưng không phân chia % traffic. Rolling with additional batch giữ capacity nhưng vẫn không phải canary thực sự. Immutable là zero-downtime nhưng cũng không split traffic.

questions.question:
A team wants `.ebextensions` to install a package from yum every time a new instance launches in their EB environment.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Create `.ebextensions/install.config` with key `packages:` then `yum:` mapping package names to versions or empty strings.
Add `apt-get install` commands to `.platform/hooks/predeploy/01_install.sh`.
Place a Dockerfile RUN directive at the root of the source bundle.
Set an environment variable EB_INSTALL_PACKAGES with comma-separated package names.

questions.explanation: `.ebextensions/*.config` (YAML) hỗ trợ key `packages.yum` (Amazon Linux), `packages.rpm`, `packages.python`. Đây là cách chuẩn cài package qua EB. B dùng cho Amazon Linux 2 platform hooks (cách mới hơn nhưng dùng `.platform/hooks/` đúng, không phải `apt-get` trên AL2). C chỉ áp dụng Docker platform. D không tồn tại.

questions.question:
A DevOps engineer needs to enforce that every pull request in CodeCommit must be approved by at least 2 senior engineers before merging to the main branch.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which combination of steps should a DevOps engineer take to meet these requirements? (Choose TWO.)

options.text: Create an Approval Rule Template specifying number of approvals = 2 and an approval pool of senior engineer IAM principals.
Associate the Approval Rule Template with the target repository (or branch via branch filter).
Enable repository-level encryption with a customer-managed KMS key.
Configure a Lambda trigger on PullRequestStateChanged events to count approvals.
Grant senior engineers the AWSCodeCommitFullAccess managed policy.

questions.explanation: CodeCommit Approval Rule Templates là feature native: (A) tạo template với rules (number of approvals, approval pool), (B) associate với repo/branch. Không cần code. C không liên quan approval flow. D là cách thủ công không cần thiết. E quyền quá rộng.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A CodeDeploy deployment to EC2 uses a Lifecycle hook 'BeforeAllowTraffic' that runs a smoke test script. The script makes an HTTPS call to https://app.internal/health which is the application running locally on the instance via ALB target group. The script consistently times out.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: BeforeAllowTraffic runs BEFORE traffic is registered with the target group, so the ALB does not route to the new task set/instance yet. Script should call localhost directly or use AfterAllowTraffic.
Lifecycle hooks have a hard 30-second timeout that cannot be extended.
BeforeAllowTraffic does not have IAM permissions to call HTTPS endpoints.
The ALB security group blocks outbound HTTPS from EC2 instances.

questions.explanation: BeforeAllowTraffic chạy SAU khi cài app nhưng TRƯỚC khi register instance vào target group, nên ALB chưa biết instance này tồn tại → call qua ALB sẽ timeout/route đến instance khác. Smoke test giai đoạn này nên gọi localhost. AfterAllowTraffic mới đúng để test qua ALB. B sai (timeout configurable). C sai (lifecycle hook scripts chạy với IAM role của instance).

questions.question:
A company wants their CodePipeline to fail if a SonarQube quality gate fails. SonarQube runs as part of a CodeBuild project. The build commands report success even when SonarQube quality gate fails because the SonarQube scanner exits 0.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Add a buildspec post_build command that calls SonarQube's `qualitygates/project_status` API and exits non-zero if status != 'OK'.
Configure CodeBuild Report Group with SonarQube format and use 'block deployment on failed report' setting.
Use CodeGuru Reviewer instead of SonarQube.
Add a CodePipeline manual approval step that requires QA to verify SonarQube before allowing the deploy stage.

questions.explanation: Pattern chuẩn: gọi API quality gate sau khi scanner xong, exit non-zero nếu fail. B không tồn tại — CodeBuild Report Groups hiện hỗ trợ JUnit, Cucumber, TestNG, NUnit, không có SonarQube native; cũng không có 'block deployment on failed report' setting tự động. C đổi sang công nghệ khác không match yêu cầu. D thêm manual gate, không tự động.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A serverless application is deployed via SAM CLI to multiple regions. The team wants `sam deploy` in CodePipeline to deploy to us-east-1, us-west-2, and eu-west-1 in parallel, with the pipeline failing if ANY region fails.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Create a single CodePipeline stage with three CodeBuild actions in parallel, each running `sam deploy` against one region. Set action runOrder to the same number so they execute concurrently.
Create three separate pipelines and chain them with EventBridge rules.
Use CloudFormation StackSets with target = three regions; one CodePipeline action triggers the StackSet operation.
Run `sam deploy` sequentially for each region in a single CodeBuild project.

questions.explanation: CodePipeline cùng `runOrder` = parallel actions trong cùng stage. Stage fail nếu bất kỳ action fail. Đây là cách native, ít code nhất. B chained pipelines = sequential, không parallel. C StackSets phù hợp CloudFormation thuần, không phải SAM (mặc dù SAM transform thành CFN nhưng `sam deploy` không gọi StackSets). D sequential không match yêu cầu parallel.

questions.question:
A team uses CodeDeploy with EC2 tag-based deployment groups. They tag instances with Environment=staging and Environment=prod. A deployment intended for staging accidentally targeted prod when an engineer typed the wrong tag value.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Use deployment configurations with stricter minimum healthy hosts.
Use a CodeDeploy 'deployment-group-trigger' to SNS for prior notification.
Configure separate IAM roles per environment and rely on IAM policy conditions; use distinct CodeDeploy applications per environment rather than per-deployment-group tag matching.
Enable CloudTrail data events on CodeDeploy.

questions.explanation: Root cause: tag-based deployment group too flexible — typing wrong tag value silently targets wrong fleet. Best practice: tách environments thành CodeDeploy applications/groups RIÊNG BIỆT + IAM policy chỉ cho phép role nào được deploy app nào (deny by default). A không liên quan target selection. B notify post-facto, không prevent. D audit log không prevent.

questions.question:
A company's CodePipeline ingests source from S3. The S3 bucket has versioning enabled. The team notices the pipeline does not auto-trigger when a new ZIP is uploaded.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: CloudTrail data events for the S3 object are not enabled, and the pipeline source action is set to 'detect changes via CloudWatch Events' which requires CloudTrail.
S3 source actions do not support auto-trigger; only CodeCommit and GitHub do.
Versioning interferes with pipeline detection.
The bucket must be in the same region as the pipeline.

questions.explanation: S3 source auto-trigger qua EventBridge yêu cầu CloudTrail data events được enable cho bucket+object — EventBridge listen 'PutObject' API calls. Nếu source action `DetectChanges = true` nhưng CloudTrail data events tắt thì pipeline không trigger. B sai (S3 auto-trigger được support). C/D không phải nguyên nhân.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. An ECS service uses CodeDeploy blue/green with ALB listener routing. Mid-deployment, the engineer realizes they want to abort and revert to blue.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.text: Stop the deployment in the CodeDeploy console with 'Stop and rollback deployment'. CodeDeploy reverts the listener to the blue target group and terminates the green task set.
Delete the green ECS task set manually.
Modify the ALB listener rule manually to point to blue, then let CodeDeploy time out.
Update the CloudFormation stack to remove the new task definition.

questions.explanation: CodeDeploy có operation 'Stop and rollback deployment' chuyên cho case này — listener về blue, green task set bị terminate gracefully. B/C/D đều bypass CodeDeploy → deployment state inconsistent, có thể block future deployments.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A buildspec.yml uses `artifacts.secondary-artifacts` to produce two artifact bundles for two downstream pipeline actions. The pipeline source action's output is named 'SourceArtifact'. The build action declares output artifacts 'ArtifactA' and 'ArtifactB'.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.text: Each downstream action's `InputArtifacts` field references either 'ArtifactA' or 'ArtifactB' by name; CodePipeline routes the corresponding secondary artifact.
Both downstream actions read 'SourceArtifact' and use a path prefix inside the bundle.
Secondary artifacts can only be consumed by Lambda invoke actions, not deploy actions.
The build phase must zip both secondary artifacts together into one named 'CombinedArtifact'.

questions.explanation: Secondary artifacts được declare trong buildspec dưới `artifacts.secondary-artifacts` và mỗi cái có identifier. Trong CodePipeline, build action output có nhiều artifacts cùng tên với identifier; downstream action reference đúng tên là routes đúng. B/C/D đều không phải behavior chuẩn.

questions.question:
A team uses CodePipeline with a CloudFormation deploy action in `CREATE_UPDATE` mode. They want the action to FAIL if the change set would replace (cause downtime to) any RDS DB instance.

The application is business critical, and failed releases must be detected quickly without requiring operators to run manual scripts on instances. The team prefers native AWS deployment features over custom tooling.
What should the DevOps engineer do to meet these requirements?

options.text: Use deployment mode `CHANGE_SET_REPLACE` + manual approval action that requires reviewer to inspect the change set summary before `CHANGE_SET_EXECUTE`.
Enable CloudFormation Drift Detection on the stack.
Use CloudFormation StackSets instead.
Set `OnFailure: ROLLBACK` in the stack policy.

questions.explanation: Pattern: tách thành 2 step — CHANGE_SET_REPLACE tạo change set (không apply), Manual Approval cho reviewer nhìn 'Replace' action trong change set summary, sau đó CHANGE_SET_EXECUTE. Không cần Lambda. B Drift Detection chỉ phát hiện thay đổi out-of-band sau khi đã apply. C/D không liên quan.

questions.question:
A DevOps engineer wants CodeBuild to fail builds whose Docker image scan reports HIGH or CRITICAL CVEs. The image is pushed to ECR.

The organization uses separate AWS accounts for tooling and workloads. The solution must work across accounts and Regions without exposing long-term credentials or public artifacts.
Which approach is the MOST appropriate?

options.text: Push the image to ECR with scan-on-push enabled. In post_build, call `aws ecr describe-image-scan-findings` and exit non-zero if HIGH/CRITICAL count > 0.
Run `docker scan` (Snyk) locally in the build container with a custom CVE policy file.
Use Amazon Inspector on the running ECS tasks and fail the build if vulnerabilities reported.
Pull a public CVE feed and grep the Dockerfile.

questions.explanation: ECR Enhanced Scanning (powered by Inspector) hoặc Basic Scanning với scan-on-push: image scan chạy ngay sau push. CodeBuild call describe-image-scan-findings, lọc severity. Đây là pattern AWS-native, ít overhead. B Snyk free tier giới hạn. C Inspector ECS scan sau khi đã deploy, quá muộn để block build. D không khả thi.

questions.question:
A company is modernizing its AWS operations. A monorepo contains 10 services. The team wants CodePipeline to trigger ONLY the affected service's pipeline when a commit touches files under that service's directory.

The deployment process is used by multiple application teams and must remain repeatable across development, staging, and production environments. The company wants to minimize custom orchestration while preserving auditability and rollback options.
